github.com/q00/ouroboros.name: Q00/ouroboros
github.com/q00/ouroboros.what: Agent OS with weighted ontology convergence at >=0.95, three-generation stagnation, period-2 oscillation, repetitive-feedback detection, checkpoints, and a 30-generation cap; latest release v0.50.4 on 2026-07-14.
github.com/q00/ouroboros.lesson: Use separate semantic stabilization, repetition, oscillation, drift, and hard-cap signals instead of one convergence score.

github.com/openhands/software-agent-sdk.name: OpenHands/software-agent-sdk
github.com/openhands/software-agent-sdk.what: SDK with a default StuckDetector for repeated action-observation pairs, repeated errors, monologues, alternating cycles, and context-window loops; latest release v1.36.0 on 2026-07-13.
github.com/openhands/software-agent-sdk.lesson: Compare normalized event content while ignoring volatile IDs, and detect both exact repeats and period-2 alternation within a bounded active-branch window.

github.com/strongdm/attractor.name: strongdm/attractor
github.com/strongdm/attractor.what: Language-agnostic coding-agent loop specification; no releases, with the latest visible commit on 2026-03-17.
github.com/strongdm/attractor.lesson: Make loop detection an observable control event that can steer the next turn, while retaining independent round, turn, and abort bounds.

github.com/aidaslab/mata.name: AIDASLab/MATA
github.com/aidaslab/mata.what: ACL 2026 multi-agent TableQA implementation; no releases, 75 commits, and an ACL 2026 paper/repository signal, with exact latest commit date not exposed.
github.com/aidaslab/mata.lesson: Gate expensive branches by confidence and agreement: skip redundant paths on agreement, escalate on disagreement or low confidence, and stop refinement on multi-metric convergence or successful execution.

github.com/egmaminta/gepa-lite.name: egmaminta/GEPA-Lite
github.com/egmaminta/gepa-lite.what: Small GEPA prompt-evolution implementation; no releases, two commits, and a GitHub 2025 update signal.
github.com/egmaminta/gepa-lite.lesson: For deliberate broadening, preserve a non-dominated Pareto pool, mix exploit/explore/merge, gate global evaluation with mini-batch non-regression, and spend full budget only on promising mutants.

github.com/google/ax.name: google/ax
github.com/google/ax.what: Early-development distributed Agent Executor runtime with event logs, snapshots, resumable streams, and isolated harnesses; latest release v0.1.0 in May 2026.
github.com/google/ax.lesson: Use a single writer and monotonic sequence numbers: clients catch up from last_seq, while incomplete conversations resume by stable conversation ID.

github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph.name: langchain-ai/langgraph
github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph.what: Stateful graph runtime for long-running agents; latest release 1.2.9 on July 10, 2026.
github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph.lesson: Parent-linked checkpoints and pending writes preserve completed parallel work, but post-checkpoint LLM and API calls replay; side effects need idempotent tasks or receipts.

github.com/temporalio/temporal.name: temporalio/temporal
github.com/temporalio/temporal.what: Durable workflow server based on ordered event histories; latest release v1.30.4 in April 2026.
github.com/temporalio/temporal.lesson: Make loop orchestration a deterministic reducer over history, isolate model and tool calls from replay, version graph changes, and compact long histories with continue-as-new.

github.com/restatedev/restate.name: restatedev/restate
github.com/restatedev/restate.what: Log-first durable runtime for workflows and AI agents; latest visible release v1.7.0-rc.2 in June 2026.
github.com/restatedev/restate.lesson: Commit each step to a journal before advancing, deduplicate with idempotency keys, fence stale attempts with epochs, and recover from snapshots plus a log suffix.

github.com/dbos-inc/dbos-transact-py.name: dbos-inc/dbos-transact-py
github.com/dbos-inc/dbos-transact-py.what: Postgres-backed durable Python workflows with step recovery and workflow administration; latest release 2.22.0 in May 2026.
github.com/dbos-inc/dbos-transact-py.lesson: Persist workflow inputs and step outputs, use stable workflow IDs for exactly-once event starts, and expose fork-from-step plus application-version gating for controlled recovery.

github.com/microsoft/agent-framework.name: microsoft/agent-framework
github.com/microsoft/agent-framework.what: Agent-native graph/workflow runtime with concurrent orchestration, custom aggregators, and fan-in barriers; latest release python-1.11.0 (2026-07-10).
github.com/microsoft/agent-framework.lesson: Separate branch completion from reduction, and preserve branch exceptions as reducer inputs.

github.com/google/adk-python.name: google/adk-python
github.com/google/adk-python.what: Agent Development Kit graph/workflow runtime with parallel agents, merger patterns, retries, and fan-out/fan-in; latest release v2.1.0 (2026-05-23).
github.com/google/adk-python.lesson: Use stable logical branch IDs and named output slots; never treat arrival order as identity.

github.com/ray-project/ray.name: ray-project/ray
github.com/ray-project/ray.what: Distributed AI/Python runtime with tasks, actors, objects, and MapReduce; latest release Ray-2.56.0 (2026-06-29).
github.com/ray-project/ray.lesson: Bound in-flight fan-out, process ready results incrementally, and distinguish crash retries from application failures.

github.com/apache/beam.name: apache/beam
github.com/apache/beam.what: Batch/stream processing model with Wait.On, GroupByKey, CoGroupByKey, Combine, windows, and triggers; latest release Beam 2.75.0 (2026-07-08).
github.com/apache/beam.lesson: Reducers need explicit algebraic and emission contracts to remain replayable and deterministic under parallel partial aggregation.

github.com/togethercomputer/moa.name: togethercomputer/MoA
github.com/togethercomputer/moa.what: Layered multi-LLM ensemble with a dedicated aggregator, configurable rounds, and parallel processes; no releases, last visible main commit Jan 7, 2025.
github.com/togethercomputer/moa.lesson: Treat synthesis as a model reducer, but gate width and layer depth by novelty gain, context growth, and token cost.

github.com/ukgovernmentbeis/inspect_ai.name: UKGovernmentBEIS/inspect_ai
github.com/ukgovernmentbeis/inspect_ai.what: UK AISI evaluation runner with solvers, model graders, multi-scorer reducers, sandbox checks, and activity through Jul 2026.
github.com/ukgovernmentbeis/inspect_ai.lesson: Persist raw per-scorer evidence and reducer outputs separately; allow rescoring existing logs without rerunning generation.

github.com/stanfordnlp/dspy.name: stanfordnlp/dspy
github.com/stanfordnlp/dspy.what: Declarative LM-program optimizer with MIPROv2 proposal, minibatch evaluation, Bayesian search, and commits through Jul 2026.
github.com/stanfordnlp/dspy.lesson: Implement improvement as proposal-evaluate-update-select with holdouts, full-validation checkpoints, and explicit trial budgets.

github.com/confident-ai/deepteam.name: confident-ai/deepteam
github.com/confident-ai/deepteam.what: LLM red-team framework with 50+ vulnerability classes, 20+ attack methods, multi-turn attack search, and commits through Jun 2026.
github.com/confident-ai/deepteam.lesson: Represent adversarial probes as first-class branches with attack lineage, vulnerability coverage, novelty, cost, and binary verdicts.

github.com/scalerlab/judgebench.name: ScalerLab/JudgeBench
github.com/scalerlab/judgebench.what: Objective-correctness benchmark and runner for pairwise LLM judges; small archived-style research repo last committed in Nov 2024.
github.com/scalerlab/judgebench.lesson: Calibrate every judge on objective held-outs, order swaps, and hard negative pairs before allowing it to control refinement.

github.com/openai/prm800k.name: openai/prm800k
github.com/openai/prm800k.what: 800,000 step-level correctness labels with labeler quality-control questions and active-learning generations; archived May 29, 2026.
github.com/openai/prm800k.lesson: Record process-level verdicts and verifier confidence, not only final outcomes; use failed steps to drive targeted repair.

github.com/noahshinn/reflexion.name: noahshinn/reflexion
github.com/noahshinn/reflexion.what: Official NeurIPS 2023 implementation with NONE, LAST_ATTEMPT, REFLEXION, persistent-memory, and resume modes; latest visible commit Jan 2025, no releases.
github.com/noahshinn/reflexion.lesson: Persist reflection as a scoped episodic artifact with attempt ID, feedback source, provenance, and explicit promotion into later iterations.

github.com/madaan/self-refine.name: madaan/self-refine
github.com/madaan/self-refine.what: Official Self-Refine implementation organized around Init, Feedback, and Iterate prompts; latest visible commit Oct 2024, no releases.
github.com/madaan/self-refine.lesson: Keep generation, critique, and revision as separate protocol stages so each can be budgeted, evaluated, replayed, or replaced independently.

github.com/ysymyth/react.name: ysymyth/ReAct
github.com/ysymyth/react.what: Official ICLR 2023 ReAct prompting code interleaving reasoning, actions, and observations; latest visible commit Jul 2023, no releases.
github.com/ysymyth/react.lesson: Represent tool intent, executed action, observation, and exception as separate replayable events; trigger reflection at evidence boundaries instead of only at loop completion.

github.com/spcl/graph-of-thoughts.name: spcl/graph-of-thoughts
github.com/spcl/graph-of-thoughts.what: Official Graph-of-Thoughts implementation using graph-of-operations, scoring, aggregation, and feedback transformations; latest visible commit Mar 2026.
github.com/spcl/graph-of-thoughts.lesson: Model reflective search as an explicit graph with merge, transform, and feedback edges rather than flattening every branch into one linear trace.

github.com/ace-agent/ace.name: ace-agent/ace
github.com/ace-agent/ace.what: ICLR 2026 Agentic Context Engineering repo with Generator, Reflector, Curator, incremental deltas, helpful/harmful counters, deduplication, pruning, and offline/online evaluation; latest visible commit May 2026, no releases.
github.com/ace-agent/ace.lesson: Use append-only memory deltas plus derived playbooks, effectiveness counters, best-checkpoint selection, and held-out regression checks to prevent context collapse.

github.com/karpathy/llm-council.name: karpathy/llm-council
github.com/karpathy/llm-council.what: Three-stage local council: independent answers, anonymous cross-review/ranking, and chairman synthesis; latest visible commit 2025.
github.com/karpathy/llm-council.lesson: Make blind peer review a separate durable event stage, with identity reveal outside adjudication.

github.com/machine-theory/lm-council.name: machine-theory/lm-council
github.com/machine-theory/lm-council.what: Council research toolkit covering 20-model subjective evaluation, jury ablations, leaderboards, and sub-council analysis; latest visible commit 2025.
github.com/machine-theory/lm-council.lesson: Treat seat selection as an experimental-design problem measured by marginal information gain per cost.

github.com/thunlp/chateval.name: thunlp/ChatEval
github.com/thunlp/chateval.what: Persona-conditioned referee team for comparing generated responses, with evidence and score outputs; latest visible commit 2024.
github.com/thunlp/chateval.lesson: Give seats explicit evaluation traits and require structured evidence before scoring or synthesis.

github.com/s-abdelnabi/llm-deliberation.name: S-Abdelnabi/LLM-Deliberation
github.com/s-abdelnabi/llm-deliberation.what: Negotiation benchmark with configurable cooperative, greedy, and adversarial incentives, multi-player variants, evaluation code, and logs; latest visible commit 2024.
github.com/s-abdelnabi/llm-deliberation.lesson: Model seat behavior through incentive and role-fidelity tests, including collusion and adversarial pressure.

github.com/henrymachiyu/multi_agent_judg.name: Henrymachiyu/Multi_Agent_Judge_Bias
github.com/henrymachiyu/multi_agent_judg.what: Official bias study with debate/meta-judge runners, raw round-by-round results, and conversation logs; 2025 paper, repository commit year not surfaced.
github.com/henrymachiyu/multi_agent_judg.lesson: Make position, verbosity, chain-of-thought, and bandwagon bias regressions explicit council gates.

github.com/mem0ai/mem0.name: mem0ai/mem0
github.com/mem0ai/mem0.what: Add-only memory extraction with entity linking, hybrid retrieval, and temporal ranking; GitHub latest release listed as v0.2.11 on Jul 13, 2026.
github.com/mem0ai/mem0.lesson: Separate append-only evidence accumulation from later conflict and validity resolution.

github.com/getzep/graphiti.name: getzep/graphiti
github.com/getzep/graphiti.what: Temporal context graph with validity windows, episode provenance, incremental updates, and hybrid retrieval; v0.29.2 released Jun 8, 2026.
github.com/getzep/graphiti.lesson: Treat contradiction and supersession as versioned knowledge events, not destructive deduplication.

github.com/microsoft/graphrag.name: microsoft/graphrag
github.com/microsoft/graphrag.what: Leiden community hierarchy with multi-level reports and local, global, and DRIFT search; v3.1.0 released May 28, 2026.
github.com/microsoft/graphrag.lesson: Track coverage at semantic-community level and broaden toward underrepresented communities instead of flat top-k retrieval.

github.com/microsoft/mnemis.name: microsoft/Mnemis
github.com/microsoft/mnemis.what: ACL 2026 repository using dual-route similarity retrieval plus top-down global selection over a hierarchical graph; 12 commits and no published release.
github.com/microsoft/mnemis.lesson: Pair a cheap near-duplicate path with an explicit global-coverage path under a shared budget.

github.com/dsail-memory/evomembench.name: DSAIL-Memory/EvoMemBench
github.com/dsail-memory/evomembench.what: Benchmark spanning in-episode versus cross-episode and knowledge versus execution memory; 9 commits, no release, associated paper revised Jun 15, 2026.
github.com/dsail-memory/evomembench.lesson: Evaluate accumulation through delayed transfer, execution reuse, interference, and token cost—not storage volume or recall alone.

github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai.name: pydantic/pydantic-ai
github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai.what: Latest release v2.9.1 on 2026-07-13; UsageLimits covers requests, input/output/total tokens, and tool calls, with per-delegation sub-agent budgets.
github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai.lesson: Make budgets hierarchical and typed by resource, with explicit soft-versus-hard exhaustion and parent-child aggregation semantics.

github.com/berriai/litellm.name: BerriAI/litellm
github.com/berriai/litellm.what: Latest release v1.92.0 on 2026-07-12; gateway supports multi-window spend limits, agent max_iterations, max_budget_per_session, and budget fallbacks.
github.com/berriai/litellm.lesson: Centralize pre-call enforcement, rolling budget windows, model-specific ceilings, and cheaper fallback routing outside individual loop implementations.

github.com/geniushtx/tale.name: GeniusHTX/TALE
github.com/geniushtx/tale.what: README's latest dated update is 2025; no release signal found; TALE estimates per-problem reasoning-token budgets through zero-shot or trained estimators.
github.com/geniushtx/tale.lesson: Allocate depth from predicted task complexity, then expose the chosen budget to the model instead of forcing every task through the same reasoning length.

github.com/sajjadanwar0/token-budgets.name: sajjadanwar0/token-budgets
github.com/sajjadanwar0/token-budgets.what: Associated with a 2026 paper; no GitHub releases published; Rust affine budget values make cloning, double-spending, and use-after-delegation compile-time errors.
github.com/sajjadanwar0/token-budgets.lesson: Treat budget ownership like a capability: reserve before fan-out, transfer ownership explicitly, and prevent concurrent branches from spending the same allocation.

github.com/microsoft/llmlingua.name: microsoft/LLMLingua
github.com/microsoft/llmlingua.what: Latest release v0.2.2 in 2024; latest visible commit is 2025-10-28; prompt and KV-cache compression supports target-token and structured-context budgets.
github.com/microsoft/llmlingua.lesson: Charge repeated state, tool output, and replay context as real cost, then compress selectively while preserving high-priority evidence and reporting quality loss.

github.com/eunomia-bpf/agentsight.name: eunomia-bpf/agentsight
github.com/eunomia-bpf/agentsight.what: eBPF boundary tracing for LLM intent, process/file effects, and live timelines; v0.1.36 released Apr 2026.
github.com/eunomia-bpf/agentsight.lesson: Add semantic and side-effect telemetry planes so gauges distinguish productive progress from activity, retries, or hidden subprocess work.

github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conve.name: open-telemetry/semantic-conventions-genai
github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conve.what: Canonical GenAI spans, metrics, and events for clients, MCP, and providers; last visible commit Jul 4 2026, no GitHub release.
github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conve.lesson: Version every JSONL record with schema metadata and validate migrations; keep spans, events, and metrics distinct.

github.com/arize-ai/openinference.name: Arize-ai/openinference
github.com/arize-ai/openinference.what: OpenTelemetry-compatible AI tracing with typed span kinds, masking, and multi-language instrumentation; latest package release Jul 1 2026.
github.com/arize-ai/openinference.lesson: Use typed operation kinds and lineage IDs, while recording model/tool configuration and redacted payload identity for replay and attribution.

github.com/langfuse/langfuse.name: langfuse/langfuse
github.com/langfuse/langfuse.what: Trace/observation/session hierarchy, scores, dataset runs, metrics, and dashboards; v3.213.0 released Jul 14 2026.
github.com/langfuse/langfuse.lesson: Keep execution records immutable and attach evaluator judgments later by trace, observation, session, or run; aggregate by release and version.

github.com/comet-ml/opik.name: comet-ml/opik
github.com/comet-ml/opik.what: Open-source tracing, online evaluation, experiment comparison, and production dashboards; v2.1.27 released Jul 14 2026.
github.com/comet-ml/opik.lesson: Expose run health, experiment deltas, cost/latency, and quality time series together instead of treating final score as convergence evidence.

github.com/stanford-oval/storm.name: stanford-oval/storm
github.com/stanford-oval/storm.what: Perspective-guided question asking plus simulated writer-expert conversations; latest release Jan 2025 and latest commit Sep 2025.
github.com/stanford-oval/storm.lesson: Seed query families from distinct perspectives, then use grounded follow-ups to expand coverage before synthesis.

github.com/langchain-ai/open_deep_resear.name: langchain-ai/open_deep_research
github.com/langchain-ai/open_deep_resear.what: Configurable deep-research agent with separate summarization, research, compression, and report stages; no releases and commits through Jun 2026.
github.com/langchain-ai/open_deep_resear.lesson: Make research stages and search providers independently configurable so cost and evidence quality can be measured per stage.

github.com/openbmb/ultrarag.name: OpenBMB/UltraRAG
github.com/openbmb/ultrarag.what: MCP-based modular RAG framework with YAML loops and branches, visible intermediate traces, and a Deep Research pipeline; latest release Apr 2026.
github.com/openbmb/ultrarag.lesson: Treat retrievers, generators, evaluators, and control flow as swappable experiment units with inspectable intermediate artifacts.

github.com/ayanami0730/arag.name: Ayanami0730/arag
github.com/ayanami0730/arag.what: Hierarchical agentic RAG with keyword, semantic, and chunk-read tools; latest commit Feb 6, 2026 and no releases.
github.com/ayanami0730/arag.lesson: Expose retrieval granularity as a policy action and track already-read chunks to deepen selectively without rereading.

github.com/sciknoworg/deep-research.name: sciknoworg/deep-research
github.com/sciknoworg/deep-research.what: Scientific deep-research implementation with explicit depth/breadth configurations and a qualitative evaluation pipeline; latest commit Apr 2026 and no releases.
github.com/sciknoworg/deep-research.lesson: Benchmark breadth/depth settings against source integration per output, not only final-answer quality.

github.com/inngest/inngest.name: inngest/inngest
github.com/inngest/inngest.what: Event-driven durable step-function and AI workflow engine; latest release v1.36.0 on Jul 8, 2026.
github.com/inngest/inngest.lesson: Stable step IDs plus memoized results let a handler replay from the top while executing only the uncommitted suffix.

github.com/hatchet-dev/hatchet.name: hatchet-dev/hatchet
github.com/hatchet-dev/hatchet.what: Postgres-backed durable tasks for background jobs and AI agents; latest release v0.94.10 on Jul 14, 2026.
github.com/hatchet-dev/hatchet.lesson: Model dynamic agent iterations as child runs, checkpoint waits and child completion, and evict waiting work without holding worker capacity.

github.com/dbos-inc/dbos-transact-golang.name: dbos-inc/dbos-transact-golang
github.com/dbos-inc/dbos-transact-golang.what: Postgres-backed durable workflows, queues, event processing, and transactions; latest release v0.19.0 on Jul 7, 2026.
github.com/dbos-inc/dbos-transact-golang.lesson: Separate at-least-once steps from exactly-once transactional boundaries, and use workflow or deduplication IDs as durable run keys.

github.com/microsoft/durabletask-go.name: microsoft/durabletask-go
github.com/microsoft/durabletask-go.what: Embeddable sidecar Durable Task engine with SQLite persistence and a gRPC boundary; last commit Mar 13, 2026 and no GitHub releases.
github.com/microsoft/durabletask-go.lesson: Keep orchestration state, executor, and storage behind a small protocol so JSONL can remain a replaceable durability backend.

github.com/langchain-ai/langgraphjs.name: langchain-ai/langgraphjs
github.com/langchain-ai/langgraphjs.what: TypeScript checkpoint base, validation suite, indexed interrupts, and ReplayState; latest visible release July 2026 with commits in July 2026.
github.com/langchain-ai/langgraphjs.lesson: Use cross-runtime checkpoint conformance tests to define semantic contracts for JSONL adapters rather than copying implementation-specific fields.

github.com/langchain-ai/agent-protocol.name: langchain-ai/agent-protocol
github.com/langchain-ai/agent-protocol.what: Thread-centric protocol with append-only history, checkpoint envelopes, reconnect sequence numbers, correlated input.respond, and batched interrupt responses; latest observed commit June 2026.
github.com/langchain-ai/agent-protocol.lesson: Treat the continuity thread as the durable cursor, keep transport sessions ephemeral, and make resume, state update, and routing decisions explicitly correlated and replayable.

github.com/langchain-ai/deepagents.name: langchain-ai/deepagents
github.com/langchain-ai/deepagents.what: Higher-level LangGraph harness combining persistence with context offloading, persistent memory, subagents, and approve/edit/reject HITL; latest release June 2026 and commits July 2026.
github.com/langchain-ai/deepagents.lesson: Keep durable execution state separate from large research artifacts and context-management state; HITL policy belongs above the checkpoint primitive.

github.com/redis-developer/langgraph-red.name: redis-developer/langgraph-redis
github.com/redis-developer/langgraph-red.what: RedisJSON/RediSearch checkpointer with separate checkpoint and write indexes, parent links, latest pointers, and relationship-aware TTL; release page shows 0.5.0 in June 2026 with a 0.5.1 release commit in July 2026.
github.com/redis-developer/langgraph-red.lesson: Retention and deletion must operate across the entire checkpoint family; expiring a snapshot while retaining writes or pointers creates orphaned continuation state.

github.com/ag2ai/ag2.name: ag2ai/ag2
github.com/ag2ai/ag2.what: AutoGen-derived AgentOS with group-chat patterns, typed handoffs, context variables, and nested orchestration.
github.com/ag2ai/ag2.lesson: Persist handoff targets, condition provenance, after-work fallbacks, and parent-pattern identity as runtime state.

github.com/crewaiinc/crewai.name: crewAIInc/crewAI
github.com/crewaiinc/crewai.what: Role-based crews with sequential or hierarchical manager processes, collaboration tools, Flows, and task guardrails.
github.com/crewaiinc/crewai.lesson: Use a manager/control plane for planning and delegation while keeping worker retries and validation local to each task.

github.com/microsoft/autogen.name: microsoft/autogen
github.com/microsoft/autogen.stars: ~59.7k; latest python-v0.7.5 released Sep 30, 2025; maintenance mode
github.com/microsoft/autogen.what: Layered agent framework with AgentChat teams, SelectorGroupChat, Swarm, GraphFlow, and Magentic-One.
github.com/microsoft/autogen.lesson: Separate adaptive conversation scheduling from deterministic graph execution, and model termination as a composable policy.

github.com/zou-group/textgrad.name: zou-group/textgrad
github.com/zou-group/textgrad.what: Textual-gradient autograd over arbitrary computation graphs; latest visible commit Jul 25, 2025.
github.com/zou-group/textgrad.lesson: Persist edge-level critique, context, target variable, and proposed update as first-class loop state.

github.com/microsoft/trace.name: microsoft/Trace
github.com/microsoft/trace.what: OPTO/OptoPrime optimizes heterogeneous workflow parameters using execution traces; latest visible commit Aug 14, 2025.
github.com/microsoft/trace.lesson: Store execution DAG slices and exception feedback with candidates so failed branches remain replayable optimization evidence.

github.com/microsoft/promptwizard.name: microsoft/PromptWizard
github.com/microsoft/promptwizard.what: Staged mutation, scoring, critique, synthesis, and example optimization; latest visible commit Aug 4, 2025.
github.com/microsoft/promptwizard.lesson: Separate instruction search from example search, retain positive and negative evidence, and gate stage transitions with explicit thresholds.

github.com/google-deepmind/opro.name: google-deepmind/opro
github.com/google-deepmind/opro.what: LLM-as-optimizer loop that feeds prior solution-score pairs into the next optimization prompt; latest visible commit Dec 4, 2024.
github.com/google-deepmind/opro.lesson: Represent optimizer history as structured, bounded trajectory state rather than an opaque conversation transcript.

github.com/meta-llama/prompt-ops.name: meta-llama/prompt-ops
github.com/meta-llama/prompt-ops.what: Model-specific prompt optimization package with measurable metrics and PDO integration; latest visible commit Dec 30, 2025.
github.com/meta-llama/prompt-ops.lesson: Use pairwise preference and bandit allocation when labels are sparse, while recording judge uncertainty and comparison cost.

github.com/swe-agent/swe-agent.name: SWE-agent/SWE-agent
github.com/swe-agent/swe-agent.what: ACI-based GitHub-issue coding agent; latest release v1.1.0 on May 22, 2025, with activity through July 2026.
github.com/swe-agent/swe-agent.lesson: Separate parser requery, full-attempt retry, reviewer selection, and total-cost termination into explicit retry scopes.

github.com/swe-agent/mini-swe-agent.name: SWE-agent/mini-swe-agent
github.com/swe-agent/mini-swe-agent.what: Minimal bash-first coding agent; latest release v2.4.5 on July 6, 2026, with commits through July 2026.
github.com/swe-agent/mini-swe-agent.lesson: Represent step, cost, wall-time, format-error, and user-interruption exits as typed terminal states, preserving the trajectory before stopping.

github.com/huggingface/smolagents.name: huggingface/smolagents
github.com/huggingface/smolagents.what: Compact ReAct/code-agent library; latest release v1.26.0 on May 29, 2026.
github.com/huggingface/smolagents.lesson: Expose step callbacks, planning checkpoints, replayable memory, and final-answer validators as stable loop-control hooks.

github.com/swe-agent/swe-rex.name: SWE-agent/SWE-ReX
github.com/swe-agent/swe-rex.what: Sandboxed local/cloud shell runtime supporting interactive sessions and parallel agents; latest release v1.4.0 in August 2025, activity through July 2026.
github.com/swe-agent/swe-rex.lesson: Decouple loop state from executor infrastructure and persist executor, session, command, exit-code, timeout, and reattachment metadata.

github.com/hyang0129/onlycodes.name: hyang0129/onlycodes
github.com/hyang0129/onlycodes.what: 2026 benchmark and MCP server comparing tool-rich coding agents with a single execute_code surface; latest release June 8, 2026.
github.com/hyang0129/onlycodes.lesson: Treat ACI/tool surface as a controlled experimental variable because path cost can change substantially without changing pass rate.

github.com/promptfoo/promptfoo.name: promptfoo/promptfoo

github.com/ukgovernmentbeis/inspect_eval.name: UKGovernmentBEIS/inspect_evals

github.com/servicenow/agentlab.name: ServiceNow/AgentLab

github.com/sierra-research/tau2-bench.name: sierra-research/tau2-bench

github.com/sierra-research/tau2-bench.lesson: Make final-state equivalence the hard verifier and keep trajectory similarity as a diagnostic signal unless the path is genuinely part of the specification.

github.com/xlang-ai/osworld.name: xlang-ai/OSWorld
github.com/xlang-ai/osworld.what: Stateful desktop/web agent benchmark with task setup, execution-based postcondition scripts, and multi-application environments; latest visible main-branch activity is 2026-06-10.
github.com/xlang-ai/osworld.lesson: For agentic work, verify externally observable state and artifacts after execution rather than trusting the final textual answer or action trace.

github.com/google/vizier.name: google/vizier
github.com/google/vizier.what: Distributed black-box optimization with an explicit early-stop policy API; recency: latest release v0.1.24 in Feb 2025, 1,236 commits, exact latest commit year not surfaced.
github.com/google/vizier.lesson: Separate global incumbent-vs-history pruning from local trajectory stopping and reconstruct policy state from persisted trial history.

github.com/optuna/optuna.name: optuna/optuna
github.com/optuna/optuna.what: Mature pruning framework with median, percentile, successive-halving, Hyperband, threshold, and patient pruners; recency: v4.9.0 released Jun 2026.
github.com/optuna/optuna.lesson: Use warm-up and comparable-depth sample requirements before pruning; treat bracket-level pruning as different from study-level termination.

github.com/zhengkid/autotts.name: zhengkid/AutoTTS
github.com/zhengkid/autotts.what: 2026 agentic test-time-scaling controller search with trend/momentum stopping, coupled width-depth allocation, and conservative branch abandonment; 4 commits and no releases.
github.com/zhengkid/autotts.lesson: Use confidence momentum and persistent disagreement instead of one-round spikes, while preserving a minimum active branch cohort.

github.com/xieyangxinyu/reasoning_uncert.name: Xieyangxinyu/reasoning_uncertainty
github.com/xieyangxinyu/reasoning_uncert.what: Reproduction code for 2026 statistical early stopping using renewal and Maxwise/conformal rules; 2 commits, no releases, latest commit date not surfaced.
github.com/xieyangxinyu/reasoning_uncert.lesson: Calibrate false-premature-stop risk and report FPR, power, calibration split, alpha, and token savings instead of exposing only a patience value.

github.com/qianjanexie/costawarestopping.name: QianJaneXie/CostAwareStoppingBayesOpt
github.com/qianjanexie/costawarestopping.what: Code for cost-aware Bayesian optimization stopping with PBGI and LogEIPC, heterogeneous evaluation costs, and convergence baselines; 311 commits, no releases, latest commit date not surfaced.
github.com/qianjanexie/costawarestopping.lesson: Replace fixed no-gain patience with a cost-aware reservation value for the best remaining evaluator or branch.

github.com/microsoft/lost_in_conversatio.name: microsoft/lost_in_conversation
github.com/microsoft/lost_in_conversatio.what: Single-turn versus multi-turn benchmark across six analytical tasks; 2025 paper/repo, no releases.
github.com/microsoft/lost_in_conversatio.lesson: Treat continuation as a risk surface; branch or reset after wrong turns and measure recovery.

github.com/microsoft/delegate52.name: microsoft/DELEGATE52
github.com/microsoft/delegate52.what: Round-trip long-horizon document-editing benchmark across 52 domains; April 2026 paper/repo, no releases.
github.com/microsoft/delegate52.lesson: Add reversible artifact canaries, per-round diffs, and rollback gates because loops can silently corrupt state.

github.com/openautocoder/agentless.name: OpenAutoCoder/Agentless
github.com/openautocoder/agentless.what: Bounded localization-repair-validation pipeline competitive on SWE-bench Lite; v1.5.0 released October 2024, latest visible commit December 2024.
github.com/openautocoder/agentless.lesson: Prefer bounded staged orchestration and deterministic validation over open-ended action loops when the task is sufficiently localized.

github.com/slhleosun/reasoning-trajector.name: slhleosun/reasoning-trajectory
github.com/slhleosun/reasoning-trajector.what: ACL 2026 code for trajectory correctness probes and gated reasoning-length interventions; no releases.
github.com/slhleosun/reasoning-trajector.lesson: Gate extra computation on correctness signals; always-on intervention can degrade accuracy while targeted intervention helps.

github.com/wzy6642/proco.name: wzy6642/ProCo
github.com/wzy6642/proco.what: Official EMNLP 2024 implementation of verify-then-correct using masked key conditions; 2024 paper/repo, no releases.
github.com/wzy6642/proco.lesson: Only admit a correction loop after identifying a falsifiable condition; treat indeterminate verification as a no-op.

github.com/texttron/hyde.name: texttron/hyde
github.com/texttron/hyde.what: Canonical HyDE reference implementation; no releases, 7 commits, and latest commit year not verified.
github.com/texttron/hyde.lesson: Represent hypothetical documents as untrusted query-control artifacts, not evidence or novelty.

github.com/raudaschl/rag-fusion.name: Raudaschl/rag-fusion
github.com/raudaschl/rag-fusion.what: Multi-query RRF evaluation harness with hybrid, reranking, bootstrap-CI, and 2026 replication experiments; exact latest commit date not exposed.
github.com/raudaschl/rag-fusion.lesson: Evaluate fusion after reranking and truncation, then route it adaptively against a baseline.

github.com/au-clan/diverge.name: au-clan/Diverge
github.com/au-clan/diverge.what: Reference implementation for iterative viewpoint-conditioned, diversity-aware agentic RAG; 20 commits, 2026 paper, no releases.
github.com/au-clan/diverge.lesson: Persist viewpoints, prior queries, answers, and retrieved embeddings so later branches target uncovered regions.

github.com/deepset-ai/haystack.name: deepset-ai/haystack
github.com/deepset-ai/haystack.what: Production RAG/agent orchestration with QueryExpander and parallel multi-query retrievers; v2.31.0 released 2026-07-08 and repo pushed 2026-07-14.
github.com/deepset-ai/haystack.lesson: Use a standard branch contract: expand, retrieve in parallel, deduplicate, preserve the original query for synthesis.

github.com/run-llama/llama_index.name: run-llama/llama_index
github.com/run-llama/llama_index.what: Agentic data framework with query transformations and fusion retrievers; v0.14.23 released 2026-06-24.
github.com/run-llama/llama_index.lesson: Make RRF a pluggable fan-in stage while retaining per-query and per-index provenance for audit.

github.com/memtensor/memos.name: MemTensor/MemOS
github.com/memtensor/memos.what: Memory OS with graph-inspectable MemCubes, asynchronous ingestion, multi-cube sharing, and feedback correction; v2.0.23 released 2026-07-09.
github.com/memtensor/memos.lesson: Represent each memory as a versioned, provenance-bearing object with explicit lifecycle states and auditable correction operations.

github.com/letta-ai/letta-code.name: letta-ai/letta-code
github.com/letta-ai/letta-code.what: Stateful memory-first agent harness with Git-tracked context, background dreaming, subagents, and self-rewriting memory; v0.28.7 released 2026-07-14.
github.com/letta-ai/letta-code.lesson: Continuity should be branchable and recoverable: snapshot memory, run maintenance asynchronously, and retain an inspectable history of context changes.

github.com/agiresearch/a-mem.name: agiresearch/A-mem
github.com/agiresearch/a-mem.what: Zettelkasten-style agentic note system that creates structured notes, links related memories, and evolves existing metadata; no releases, last visible commit in 2025.
github.com/agiresearch/a-mem.lesson: Store notes as typed claims with explicit links, tags, provenance, and supersession edges instead of flattening every iteration into one summary.

github.com/llm-vlm-gsl/ariadnemem.name: LLM-VLM-GSL/AriadneMem
github.com/llm-vlm-gsl/ariadnemem.what: 2026 research implementation using entropy gating, conflict-aware coarsening, temporal state-update edges, bridge discovery, and topology-aware synthesis; no releases.
github.com/llm-vlm-gsl/ariadnemem.lesson: Separate offline memory construction from online reasoning, merge duplicates, preserve state changes as temporal edges, and use algorithmic graph bridging before spending another LLM call.

github.com/aiming-lab/simplemem.name: aiming-lab/SimpleMem
github.com/aiming-lab/simplemem.what: Lifelong-memory stack combining semantic compression, online consolidation, query-aware retrieval, and EvolveMem optimization; v0.3.0 released 2026-05-21, with commits in June 2026.
github.com/aiming-lab/simplemem.lesson: Use write-time compression and consolidation to control memory growth, but retain raw JSONL lineage so summaries remain reversible and testable.

github.com/agentscope-ai/agentscope.name: agentscope-ai/agentscope
github.com/agentscope-ai/agentscope.what: MsgHub plus sequential and fanout pipelines; v2.0.4 released Jul 7, 2026.
github.com/agentscope-ai/agentscope.lesson: Keep fan-out transport separate from reduction: return branch envelopes first, then apply an explicit reducer or synthesis node with deterministic ordering policy.

github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go.name: trpc-group/trpc-agent-go
github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go.what: Graph workflows with dynamic fan-out and typed StateSchema reducers; v1.10.0 released Jun 5, 2026.
github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go.lesson: Define reducers per state field: append findings, sum counters, preserve execution history, and retain partial-failure metadata instead of using last-write-wins.

github.com/aixgo-dev/aixgo.name: aixgo-dev/aixgo
github.com/aixgo-dev/aixgo.what: Go framework documenting aggregation, ensemble, hierarchical, and MapReduce patterns; v0.7.4 released May 2, 2026.
github.com/aixgo-dev/aixgo.lesson: Make reducer strategy configurable: consensus, weighted, semantic, hierarchical, and RAG-based policies should be runtime data rather than hard-coded synthesis behavior.

github.com/langchain-ai/langchain.name: langchain-ai/langchain
github.com/langchain-ai/langchain.what: Classic map/reduce document chains support recursive collapse, token_max, and collapse retry limits; langchain-core 1.4.9 released Jul 8, 2026.
github.com/langchain-ai/langchain.lesson: Use a bounded reduction tree: collapse intermediate branch summaries until they fit the final reducer budget, checkpointing each collapse level and preserving provenance.

github.com/yuchenlin/llm-blender.name: yuchenlin/LLM-Blender
github.com/yuchenlin/llm-blender.what: ACL2023 PairRanker plus GenFuser; no releases, latest visible commit Oct 22, 2024.
github.com/yuchenlin/llm-blender.lesson: Split reducer admission from synthesis: rank or filter candidates first, then let a synthesizer see only the strongest diverse candidates and their scores.

github.com/aws/aws-durable-execution-sdk.name: aws/aws-durable-execution-sdk-python
github.com/aws/aws-durable-execution-sdk.what: AWS Lambda durable-functions SDK; latest release July 9, 2026.
github.com/aws/aws-durable-execution-sdk.lesson: Expose at-least-once versus at-most-once per retry, and persist operation identity inside checkpointed steps.

github.com/triggerdotdev/trigger.dev.name: triggerdotdev/trigger.dev
github.com/triggerdotdev/trigger.dev.what: Durable AI workflow platform; latest release July 14, 2026.
github.com/triggerdotdev/trigger.dev.lesson: Make idempotency scope explicit: run-scoped deduplication, attempt-scoped replay, or global once-only behavior.

github.com/conductor-oss/conductor.name: conductor-oss/conductor
github.com/conductor-oss/conductor.what: Durable agentic workflow engine; latest release June 25, 2026.
github.com/conductor-oss/conductor.lesson: Separate restart, rerun-from-task, retry-from-failure, and compensation workflows instead of using one generic resume operation.

github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows.name: argoproj/argo-workflows
github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows.what: Kubernetes workflow engine; latest release July 7, 2026.
github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows.lesson: Retry decisions should be conditional on failure metadata, while locks require shared storage, namespaces, queues, and multi-controller recovery.

github.com/mastra-ai/mastra.name: mastra-ai/mastra
github.com/mastra-ai/mastra.what: TypeScript agent and workflow framework; release feed current through July 8, 2026.
github.com/mastra-ai/mastra.lesson: Parallel resume must merge branch-local snapshots without clearing sibling suspension payloads or retry budgets.

github.com/openai/openai-agents-python.name: openai/openai-agents-python
github.com/openai/openai-agents-python.what: Approval-aware multi-agent SDK; latest release v0.18.2 on 2026-07-11.
github.com/openai/openai-agents-python.lesson: Persist pending decisions, nested-agent identity, usage, and context merge semantics, then resume from the same agent graph.

github.com/cloudflare/agents.name: cloudflare/agents
github.com/cloudflare/agents.what: Durable-Object agent/workflow runtime; latest listed release @cloudflare/think@0.13.0 on 2026-07-13.
github.com/cloudflare/agents.lesson: Represent approval as a durable external wait with actor identity, progress, timeout, escalation, and multi-approver quorum.

github.com/ag-ui-protocol/ag-ui.name: ag-ui-protocol/ag-ui
github.com/ag-ui-protocol/ag-ui.what: Event-based agent-user interaction protocol; latest release listed as 2026-07-03.
github.com/ag-ui-protocol/ag-ui.lesson: Make pause, approve, edit, retry, escalate, cancel, and steering typed events with event-sourced state diffs and conflict resolution.

github.com/researai/deepscientist.name: ResearAI/DeepScientist
github.com/researai/deepscientist.what: Durable research workspace with human takeover; latest release v1.6.0 on 2026-05-13.
github.com/researai/deepscientist.lesson: Treat plans, files, graph state, memory, execution history, and failed paths as the continuity thread that humans can edit before resuming.

github.com/agentscope-ai/agentteams.name: agentscope-ai/AgentTeams
github.com/agentscope-ai/agentteams.what: Manager-worker multi-agent runtime with human-visible Matrix coordination; latest release v1.1.2 on 2026-05-27.
github.com/agentscope-ai/agentteams.lesson: Put humans, managers, workers, and artifacts in one auditable control room; make steering durable room events rather than hidden side-channel prompts.

github.com/microsoft/best-route-llm.name: microsoft/best-route-llm
github.com/microsoft/best-route-llm.what: ICML 2025 code for routing queries jointly by model tier and best-of-N sample count.
github.com/microsoft/best-route-llm.lesson: Treat model tier, branch count, and loop depth as one priced scheduling action.

github.com/junhongmit/p-and-b.name: junhongmit/P-and-B
github.com/junhongmit/p-and-b.what: ICLR 2026 Plan-and-Budget implementation with decomposition, local token scheduling, BAM, and E3.
github.com/junhongmit/p-and-b.lesson: Split global budgets into uncertainty-weighted subquestion or branch budgets, then reallocate unused capacity.

github.com/pranjal2041/adaptiveconsisten.name: Pranjal2041/AdaptiveConsistency
github.com/pranjal2041/adaptiveconsisten.what: EMNLP 2023 library using Beta or Dirichlet posterior stability to stop sampling early.
github.com/pranjal2041/adaptiveconsisten.lesson: Use calibrated sequential consensus at fan-in, with minimum diversity or verification floors against correlated errors.

github.com/simplescaling/s1.name: simplescaling/s1
github.com/simplescaling/s1.what: 2025 test-time scaling code implementing budget forcing by truncating reasoning or appending continuation prompts.
github.com/simplescaling/s1.lesson: Represent budget exhaustion as an explicit stop, summarize, or continue state transition rather than an opaque kill.

github.com/raymin0223/mixture_of_recursi.name: raymin0223/mixture_of_recursions
github.com/raymin0223/mixture_of_recursi.what: NeurIPS 2025 model that learns per-token recursive depth and recursion-aware KV caching.
github.com/raymin0223/mixture_of_recursi.lesson: Separate inner-model recursion depth from outer loop iterations and account for cache and batching costs.

github.com/microsoft/agent-lightning.name: microsoft/agent-lightning
github.com/microsoft/agent-lightning.what: Framework-agnostic agent training control plane with rollouts, retryable attempts, spans, workers, and versioned resources; latest release v0.3.0 in Dec 2025.
github.com/microsoft/agent-lightning.lesson: Make rollout identity stable across retries, separate attempt state from aggregate state, and use monotonic event sequencing for distributed fan-in.

github.com/github/gh-aw.name: github/gh-aw
github.com/github/gh-aw.what: Compiles natural-language Markdown workflows into hardened GitHub Actions; latest release v0.81.6 in Jun 2026.
github.com/github/gh-aw.lesson: Compile mutable intent into a locked execution artifact, isolate read-only reasoning from approved writes, and enforce turns, time, and credit ceilings outside the model.

github.com/mas-infra-layer/agent-git.name: MAS-Infra-Layer/Agent-Git

github.com/mas-infra-layer/agent-git.lesson: A checkpoint is not a rollback unless external effects have inverse or compensating actions; preserve original branches when applying late human edits.

github.com/servicenow/browsergym.name: ServiceNow/BrowserGym
github.com/servicenow/browsergym.what: Gym-style interactive web-agent environment with unified task contracts and traceable benchmarks; latest release v0.14.3 in Jan 2026.
github.com/servicenow/browsergym.lesson: Define loop steps through explicit observation, action, reward, termination, and truncation fields so replay and cross-agent evaluation remain comparable.

github.com/ethz-spylab/agentdojo.name: ethz-spylab/agentdojo
github.com/ethz-spylab/agentdojo.what: Dynamic prompt-injection attack/defense benchmark for tool-using agents; latest release v0.1.35 in Oct 2025.
github.com/ethz-spylab/agentdojo.lesson: Score task utility and security independently; a successful answer must not compensate for an unsafe tool transition or policy violation.

github.com/sakanaai/treequest.name: SakanaAI/treequest
github.com/sakanaai/treequest.what: AB-MCTS tree-search library that adaptively widens or deepens; latest release v0.3.2 in February 2026.
github.com/sakanaai/treequest.lesson: Make widen versus deepen a per-frontier-node budget action, not a run-wide mode selected only after convergence.

github.com/hao-ai-lab/dynasor.name: hao-ai-lab/Dynasor
github.com/hao-ai-lab/dynasor.what: NeurIPS 2025 reasoning server using Certaindex answer stabilization for dynamic early exit; no formal GitHub releases.
github.com/hao-ai-lab/dynasor.lesson: Treat stability as a branch-level compute-allocation signal; never equate unchanged answers with global correctness or frontier exhaustion.

github.com/vainf/thinkless.name: VainF/Thinkless
github.com/vainf/thinkless.what: NeurIPS 2025 adaptive short-versus-long reasoning implementation; latest visible commit was September 2025.
github.com/vainf/thinkless.lesson: Add a pre-loop depth-admission policy based on task difficulty and model ability, separate from within-loop convergence.

github.com/scalingintelligence/large_lan.name: ScalingIntelligence/large_language_monkeys
github.com/scalingintelligence/large_lan.what: Code for repeated-sampling inference scaling; a three-commit 2024 research artifact with no releases, and exact last-commit date was not exposed.
github.com/scalingintelligence/large_lan.lesson: Breadth can keep increasing oracle coverage while practical selectors plateau, so broadening must be gated by verifier capability and selection regret.

github.com/automl/dehb.name: automl/DEHB
github.com/automl/dehb.what: Evolutionary Hyperband combines diverse population search with cheap low-fidelity pruning; v0.1.2 released July 2024 and now maintenance-only.
github.com/automl/dehb.lesson: Reconcile breadth and full depth through successive promotion: probe many branches cheaply, preserve diversity, and fully fund only survivors plus calibration sentinels.

github.com/apache/spark.name: apache/spark
github.com/apache/spark.what: Large-scale data engine with explicit DETERMINATE, UNORDERED, and INDETERMINATE replay classes; Spark 4.1.2 released May 2026.
github.com/apache/spark.lesson: Attach a determinism class to every fan-out stage and compare ordered versus multiset digests according to that contract.

github.com/dask/distributed.name: dask/distributed
github.com/dask/distributed.what: Distributed scheduler exposing completion-order iteration, input-order gathering, and content-derived keys; latest GitHub release dated July 2026.
github.com/dask/distributed.lesson: Separate arrival order from canonical reduction order, and separate deterministic work identity from stochastic sample identity.

github.com/prefecthq/prefect.name: PrefectHQ/prefect
github.com/prefecthq/prefect.what: Python workflow engine whose cache isolation documents concurrent same-key execution under READ_COMMITTED; release 3.7.8 in July 2026.
github.com/prefecthq/prefect.lesson: A matching cache key is not mutual exclusion; novelty reuse needs a serialized claim or atomic compare-and-set.

github.com/hibiken/asynq.name: hibiken/asynq
github.com/hibiken/asynq.what: Redis-backed Go task queue using atomic uniqueness scripts and owner-checked lock deletion; v0.26.0 released February 2026.
github.com/hibiken/asynq.lesson: Store owner and generation with each dedup claim, and release it only through an owner-checked fenced transition.

github.com/uxlfoundation/onetbb.name: uxlfoundation/oneTBB
github.com/uxlfoundation/onetbb.what: C++ parallel runtime with a deterministic reduction primitive that fixes split and join topology; latest GitHub release dated July 2026.
github.com/uxlfoundation/onetbb.lesson: Version and replay the reduction tree itself; canonical input ordering alone does not stabilize non-associative aggregation.

github.com/i-eval/faireval.name: i-Eval/FairEval
github.com/i-eval/faireval.what: Position-bias calibration artifact implementing multiple-evidence and balanced-position evaluation; last commit September 2023.
github.com/i-eval/faireval.lesson: Execute A/B and B/A as one review transaction, record flip rate and diversity entropy, and abstain or escalate instead of silently averaging inconsistent judgments.

github.com/tatsu-lab/alpaca_eval.name: tatsu-lab/alpaca_eval
github.com/tatsu-lab/alpaca_eval.what: Automatic evaluator with randomized presentation and counterfactual length-controlled win rates; last commit August 2025.
github.com/tatsu-lab/alpaca_eval.lesson: Persist raw and length-controlled rankings together and block convergence when verbosity controls change the selected winner.

github.com/microsoft/llm-rubric.name: microsoft/LLM-Rubric
github.com/microsoft/llm-rubric.what: Multidimensional rubric implementation that calibrates LLM response distributions to individual human judges; last commit February 2025.
github.com/microsoft/llm-rubric.lesson: Treat criterion outputs as probabilistic features calibrated on human annotations, including judge-specific parameters, rather than directly averaging nominal rubric scores.

github.com/neuir/genii.name: NEUIR/Genii
github.com/neuir/genii.what: ACL 2026 research artifact for group-polling optimization against judgment self-preference; repository updated January 2026.
github.com/neuir/genii.lesson: Rotate proposer and judge roles and build cross-family consistency checks; runtime-only role rotation is an adaptation because the reported method also performs model optimization.

github.com/s3ic-lab/robustjudge.name: S3IC-Lab/RobustJudge
github.com/s3ic-lab/robustjudge.what: Judge-robustness harness covering 15 attacks and 7 defenses across 12 models; last commit September 2025.
github.com/s3ic-lab/robustjudge.lesson: Gate evaluator changes on adversarial canaries and preserve clean, attacked, and defended judgments in JSONL so every score change is replayable.

github.com/terrierteam/ir_measures.name: terrierteam/ir_measures
github.com/terrierteam/ir_measures.what: Unified IR evaluation library implementing alpha_nDCG, alpha_DCG, ERR_IA and other intent-aware measures; latest visible release v0.4.3 in November 2025.
github.com/terrierteam/ir_measures.lesson: Represent findings as ranked aspect-bearing evidence and exponentially discount repeated hits to already-covered aspects instead of self-scoring novelty holistically.

github.com/decile-team/submodlib.name: decile-team/submodlib
github.com/decile-team/submodlib.what: Submodular data-summarization library with facility-location, set-cover and explicit marginalGain operations; PyPI v0.0.3 provenance points to a May 2025 repository tag.
github.com/decile-team/submodlib.lesson: Score every prospective pivot by marginal coverage gain under the remaining budget; diminishing returns make re-affirming directions naturally lose priority.

github.com/scikit-bio/scikit-bio.name: scikit-bio/scikit-bio
github.com/scikit-bio/scikit-bio.what: Active bioinformatics library implementing Good's coverage and Chao1 richness estimation; latest release v0.7.3 in June 2026.
github.com/scikit-bio/scikit-bio.lesson: Use singleton/doubleton semantic-cluster counts to estimate unseen topic mass, separating local exhaustion from global coverage.

github.com/guilgautier/dppy.name: guilgautier/DPPy
github.com/guilgautier/dppy.what: Python toolbox for determinantal point-process sampling; the repository exposes 462 commits, but a current dated release or latest-commit year was not verifiable from the available page.
github.com/guilgautier/dppy.lesson: Select fan-out branches jointly with a quality-weighted k-DPP so the batch spans distinct evidence regions rather than producing correlated restatements.

github.com/dapr/dapr-agents.name: dapr/dapr-agents
github.com/dapr/dapr-agents.what: Workflow-backed agent framework; v1.0.5 released 2026-06-15; records LLM activity outputs for replay.
github.com/dapr/dapr-agents.lesson: Put each LLM/tool call behind a durable activity boundary and replay the committed response rather than invoking the provider again.

github.com/get-convex/workflow.name: get-convex/workflow
github.com/get-convex/workflow.what: TypeScript durable-workflow component; v0.4.4 tagged 2026-06-10; deterministic replay with explicit 8 MiB journal limits.
github.com/get-convex/workflow.lesson: Stable step identities and workflow versions are part of the history schema; regenerating from an earlier step creates a new epoch rather than transparent recovery.

github.com/kurrent-io/kurrentdb.name: kurrent-io/KurrentDB
github.com/kurrent-io/kurrentdb.what: Event-native append-only database; v26.1.0 released 2026-04-30; supports atomic appends, expected-revision checks, and event-ID idempotency.
github.com/kurrent-io/kurrentdb.lesson: Require expected_seq and deterministic event_id on every canonical JSONL transition so retries are idempotent and stale writers fail closed.

github.com/hazelcast/hazelcast.name: hazelcast/hazelcast
github.com/hazelcast/hazelcast.what: Distributed data platform with CP FencedLock; v5.7.0 released 2026-05-13.
github.com/hazelcast/hazelcast.lesson: A lease must yield a monotonic fencing epoch that every state sink and effect broker validates; the lock service alone cannot stop a paused stale worker.

github.com/nanomaoli/llm_reproducibility.name: nanomaoli/llm_reproducibility
github.com/nanomaoli/llm_reproducibility.what: Code for NeurIPS 2025 inference-reproducibility research; no formal releases, with repository updates through 2026-05-29.
github.com/nanomaoli/llm_reproducibility.lesson: Seed, temperature, and model name are provenance rather than replay guarantees; persist the actual response because hardware and batching can alter greedy reasoning.

github.com/rlhflow/self-rewarding-reason.name: RLHFlow/Self-rewarding-reasoning-LLM
github.com/rlhflow/self-rewarding-reason.what: Training and evaluation recipes for self-rewarding correction; last default-branch commit 2025-03-02.
github.com/rlhflow/self-rewarding-reason.lesson: Measure wrong-to-correct and correct-to-wrong separately; prompted correction hurt badly while trained correction produced net gains.

github.com/whgtyen/big-bench-mistake.name: WHGTyen/BIG-Bench-Mistake
github.com/whgtyen/big-bench-mistake.what: First-error-location benchmark over five reasoning tasks; five commits and last push in 2024.
github.com/whgtyen/big-bench-mistake.lesson: Split reflection into locate_error, validate_location, and repair_from_boundary instead of permitting an unconstrained rewrite.

github.com/open-compass/criticeval.name: open-compass/CriticEval
github.com/open-compass/criticeval.what: NeurIPS 2024 benchmark separating feedback, comparison, correction, and meta-feedback; last commit 2024-11-29.
github.com/open-compass/criticeval.lesson: Calibrate critic capabilities separately; good critique prose or ranking ability does not imply safe revision.

github.com/princeton-pli/contextual-drag.name: princeton-pli/Contextual-Drag
github.com/princeton-pli/contextual-drag.what: 2026 code for an 11-model, eight-task study of failed drafts contaminating later reasoning; 11 commits and no releases.
github.com/princeton-pli/contextual-drag.lesson: Quarantine rejected rationales and send only structured failure evidence into a clean-context repair branch.

github.com/deeplearning-wisc/debate-or-v.name: deeplearning-wisc/debate-or-vote
github.com/deeplearning-wisc/debate-or-v.what: NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight code separating majority-vote gains from inter-agent debate; latest commit year was not surfaced.
github.com/deeplearning-wisc/debate-or-v.lesson: Run independent proposals plus voting first, and spend debate rounds only when a targeted correction signal exists.

github.com/instadeepai/debatellm.name: instadeepai/DebateLLM
github.com/instadeepai/debatellm.what: Benchmark suite for cost, latency, accuracy, agreement, and protocol comparisons behind the 2023 Should we be going MAD? study; latest commit year was not verified.
github.com/instadeepai/debatellm.lesson: Treat council protocols as tunable policies benchmarked against self-consistency and ensembling, not as inherently superior architectures.

github.com/dinobby/reconcile.name: dinobby/ReConcile
github.com/dinobby/reconcile.what: Official ACL 2024 code for heterogeneous round-table reasoning with confidence-weighted consensus; latest commit year was not verified.
github.com/dinobby/reconcile.lesson: Use heterogeneous seats and reliability-aware aggregation, while calibrating confidence against observed accuracy or external evidence.

github.com/da2i2-slm/dar.name: DA2I2-SLM/DAR
github.com/da2i2-slm/dar.what: March 2026 preprint implementation of diversity-aware message retention for efficient multi-agent debate; repository stars and latest commit were not independently surfaced.
github.com/da2i2-slm/dar.lesson: Fan-in should retain representative disagreement rather than broadcasting every redundant council message.

github.com/thinking-machines-lab/batch_i.name: thinking-machines-lab/batch_invariant_ops
github.com/thinking-machines-lab/batch_i.what: Batch-invariant PyTorch/Triton kernels plus deterministic-vLLM demonstration; released with the 2025 determinism work and indexed in 2026.
github.com/thinking-machines-lab/batch_i.lesson: Seed capture cannot repair logits changed by dynamic batching; strict live replay needs batch-invariant kernels or recorded model outputs, with the throughput penalty exposed as replay cost.

github.com/vllm-project/vllm.name: vllm-project/vllm
github.com/vllm-project/vllm.what: High-throughput LLM inference engine whose reproducibility contract is limited to pinned hardware/version and deterministic offline scheduling; v0.20.0 appeared in 2026 search results.
github.com/vllm-project/vllm.lesson: Record an explicit reproducibility capability per execution: exact, same-environment-only, best-effort, or unsupported; online serving should normally be marked best-effort.

github.com/pyeventsourcing/eventsourcing.name: pyeventsourcing/eventsourcing
github.com/pyeventsourcing/eventsourcing.what: Python event-sourcing library with snapshots, event/schema versioning, upcasting, projections, encryption, compression, and optimistic concurrency; GitHub showed a 5,377-commit history in 2026.
github.com/pyeventsourcing/eventsourcing.lesson: Treat JSONL as immutable evidence, version reducers and event schemas, and make snapshots verified acceleration points rather than replacements for replayable history.

github.com/vcr/vcr.name: vcr/vcr
github.com/vcr/vcr.what: HTTP interaction cassette recorder/replayer for deterministic tests; repository was indexed in 2026, but the latest release or commit year was not verified.
github.com/vcr/vcr.lesson: Model and tool calls can be represented as replayable effects keyed by canonical request hashes, giving zero-token deterministic orchestration tests without claiming current-model equivalence.

github.com/meta-pytorch/botorch.name: meta-pytorch/botorch
github.com/meta-pytorch/botorch.what: Bayesian-optimization primitives including multi-fidelity knowledge gradient and information-gain-per-cost; v0.17.2 released March 2026.
github.com/meta-pytorch/botorch.lesson: Fit a posterior over iteration payoff and continue only while optimistic information gain per token or wall-clock cost exceeds a reservation threshold.

github.com/syne-tune/syne-tune.name: syne-tune/syne-tune
github.com/syne-tune/syne-tune.what: Multi-fidelity optimization library implementing ASHA, Hyperband, cost-aware search, pause/resume, and global stopping criteria; v0.15.0 released December 2025.
github.com/syne-tune/syne-tune.lesson: Treat parallel loop lineages as multi-fidelity trials: grant a grace budget, compare them at common rungs, prune weak lineages, and promote promising ones.

github.com/fidelity/mabwiser.name: fidelity/mabwiser
github.com/fidelity/mabwiser.what: Contextual multi-armed-bandit library with UCB, Thompson sampling, neighborhood policies, parallel evaluation, and simulation; v2.7.4 released September 2024.
github.com/fidelity/mabwiser.lesson: Model unresolved questions or improvement strategies as contextual arms, reward verified gain per unit cost, and stop when no arm's optimistic index clears the global compute price.

github.com/stanford-futuredata/frugalgpt.name: stanford-futuredata/FrugalGPT
github.com/stanford-futuredata/frugalgpt.what: Budget-aware LLM cascade research and evaluation code; latest visible commits and model updates are from February 2025.
github.com/stanford-futuredata/frugalgpt.lesson: Run a cheap pass first, estimate whether its result is adequate, and escalate to another iteration or stronger executor only when expected quality gain warrants the added cost.

github.com/ukgovernmentbeis/control-aren.name: UKGovernmentBEIS/control-arena
github.com/ukgovernmentbeis/control-aren.what: Official AISI framework for paired honest-versus-attack AI-control evaluations and post-hoc macro-protocol analysis; official launch and v2.1.0 package are visible from 2025, but GitHub stars and exact latest commit were not retrievable.
github.com/ukgovernmentbeis/control-aren.lesson: Test councils as adversarial control protocols: run matched honest and attack conditions, vary compromised-seat topology, and measure both safety and usefulness rather than agreement alone.

github.com/gostevehoward/confseq.name: gostevehoward/confseq
github.com/gostevehoward/confseq.what: Reference implementation of time-uniform confidence sequences and uniform boundaries; the latest package release surfaced by search is from 2022, while GitHub stars and latest commit year were not verified.
github.com/gostevehoward/confseq.lesson: Replace repeated fixed-horizon confidence tests with an anytime-valid gate, but encode the filtration, predictable selection rule, and shared-model dependence assumptions explicitly.

github.com/st-tech/zr-obp.name: st-tech/zr-obp
github.com/st-tech/zr-obp.what: Open Bandit Pipeline implements IPW, direct-method, doubly robust, and other off-policy estimators over logged bandit data; GitHub lists about 708 stars and release 0.5.5 from June 2022.
github.com/st-tech/zr-obp.lesson: Make future stop-policy learning identifiable by logging the eligible actions, selected action, behavior propensity, cost, outcome, and censoring state at every loop decision.

github.com/codenotary/immudb.name: codenotary/immudb
github.com/codenotary/immudb.what: Cryptographically coherent immutable database with verifiable reads and structured JSON audit events; the current repository page references v1.9.5 and recent audit-log work, but stars and exact latest release date were not verified.
github.com/codenotary/immudb.lesson: Keep the replay ledger immutable but move sensitive content into per-record encrypted sidecars; chain ciphertext commitments and redaction events, then erase payload keys without rewriting history.

github.com/rr-debugger/rr.name: rr-debugger/rr
github.com/rr-debugger/rr.what: Linux process-and-thread record/replay debugger that captures nondeterministic execution inputs; GitHub lists about 10.5k stars and release 5.9.0 from February 2025.
github.com/rr-debugger/rr.lesson: Treat exact replay as boundary capture, not seed reuse: record provider bytes, tool results, clocks, environment, scheduling-relevant events, and a state root before permitting a live counterfactual suffix.

github.com/leaplabthu/absolute-zero-reas.name: LeapLabTHU/Absolute-Zero-Reasoner
github.com/leaplabthu/absolute-zero-reas.what: Self-play reasoning loop (PROPOSE→SOLVE) with learnability reward — tasks rewarded only if they improve the model, not just if correct
github.com/leaplabthu/absolute-zero-reas.lesson: Convergence signal should measure downstream performance delta (did this iteration actually help?), not just output correctness or novelty

github.com/mll-lab-nu/ragen.name: mll-lab-nu/RAGEN
github.com/mll-lab-nu/ragen.what: StarPO agent RL framework with template-collapse diagnostics (MI proxy H(Z|X) vs I(X;Z)) and SNR-adaptive filtering by reward variance
github.com/mll-lab-nu/ragen.lesson: Diversity metrics based on entropy alone miss template collapse — decompose into within-iteration diversity and cross-iteration distinguishability; skip zero-variance iterations to save budget

github.com/langfengq/verl-agent.name: langfengQ/verl-agent
github.com/langfengq/verl-agent.what: GiGPO: two-level credit assignment (episode-level + step-level groups) for long-horizon agent RL; step-independent rollout with customizable memory module keeping context constant over 30-50 steps
github.com/langfengq/verl-agent.lesson: Nested grouping for fan-out (iteration-level + sub-task-level) and memory-module pattern for checkpointing long-horizon loops without unbounded context growth

github.com/verl-project/verl.name: verl-project/verl
github.com/verl-project/verl.what: HybridFlow RL library with experimental fully_async_policy, one_step_off_policy, and transfer_queue modules for bounded-staleness async RL
github.com/verl-project/verl.lesson: Async fan-out needs an explicit staleness bound (one-step window) plus a transfer queue that buffers and orders stale samples before merge

github.com/openrlhf/openrlhf.name: OpenRLHF/OpenRLHF
github.com/openrlhf/openrlhf.what: RLHF framework with off-policy correction (TIS token-clamp / ICEPOP token-filter) via importance sampling thresholds [low,high] for async rollouts
github.com/openrlhf/openrlhf.lesson: Fan-in should weight parallel results by a freshness/importance-sampling score and discard iterations whose state drift exceeds a bounded threshold

github.com/apache/airflow.name: apache/airflow
github.com/apache/airflow.what: The canonical DAG scheduler; Pools (named semaphores), SensorOperators, backfills over (task,partition) grids, max_retries default of 3.
github.com/apache/airflow.lesson: Backfill = idempotent per-partition recovery (not monolithic replay); Pools = named per-resource concurrency gating; sensors must release their slot (reschedule mode) or the pool deadlocks.

github.com/dagster-io/dagster.name: dagster-io/dagster
github.com/dagster-io/dagster.what: Asset-centric orchestration; partition-based backfills as a first-class primitive, sensors, and per-task concurrency keys.
github.com/dagster-io/dagster.lesson: Partition-keyed backfill over software-defined assets is the cleanest 'recover only the stale slice' model; concurrency keys generalize pools to per-resource semaphores.

github.com/netflix/metaflow.name: Netflix/metaflow
github.com/netflix/metaflow.what: Netflix ML orchestration; foreach fan-out/fan-in with implicit reduce, per-step immutable artifact versioning, resume-from-any-step.
github.com/netflix/metaflow.lesson: Fan-out branches must emit content-addressed artifacts read by reference by the reducer, enabling cheap partial re-execution and resume without recomputing completed siblings.

github.com/flyteorg/flyte.name: flyteorg/flyte
github.com/flyteorg/flyte.what: Kubernetes-native dynamic workflow engine; map tasks emit a runtime-determined fan width scheduled by the control plane.
github.com/flyteorg/flyte.lesson: Fan width should be a runtime decision bounded by a pool, not pre-declared at compile time; the control plane (not the loop body) owns scheduling and retries.

github.com/spotify/luigi.name: spotify/luigi
github.com/spotify/luigi.what: Airflow's predecessor; target-based idempotency (a task re-runs only if its output target is absent) is the original 'do I need to recompute?' gate.
github.com/spotify/luigi.lesson: Idempotency should be enforced by checking an output-target existence predicate, not by the caller remembering — a target-exists check is the cheapest dedup/locks gate.

github.com/dotnet/orleans.name: dotnet/orleans
github.com/dotnet/orleans.what: Virtual Actor Model: stateful grains addressed by stable identity, auto-activated on demand, auto-deactivated when idle, with durable reminders (timers that survive deactivation)
github.com/dotnet/orleans.lesson: Fan-out workers addressed by stable identity get transparent failure recovery, idle-resource reclamation, and natural resume-on-revisit for free — the activation boundary IS the checkpoint boundary

github.com/apache/flink.name: apache/flink
github.com/apache/flink.what: Stream processor with Chandy-Lamport async barrier snapshotting (consistent distributed snapshots without stop-the-world), iterate operator for bounded-stream loops, pluggable state backends
github.com/apache/flink.lesson: Checkpoint-barrier injection is the formal mechanism for consistent-cut snapshots across concurrent fan-out workers — but its coordination cost is only net-positive at high partition counts

github.com/apache/kafka.name: apache/kafka
github.com/apache/kafka.what: Kafka Streams: local state stores (RocksDB) backed by changelog topics for automatic recovery; exactly-once via transactional consume-produce-commit in one transaction
github.com/apache/kafka.lesson: State-write + output-emission + checkpoint-advance must be one atomic transaction (all-or-nothing) or retry causes double-processing — the dual hot-store + changelog model with transactional commit

github.com/apache/pekko.name: apache/pekko
github.com/apache/pekko.what: Akka fork (pre-BSL): actor model with Persistence (append-only journal + periodic snapshots, recovery replays events since last snapshot), persistence-query CQRS read-side, cluster sharding by entity-ID hash
github.com/apache/pekko.lesson: Periodic snapshots collapse resume replay from O(iterations) to O(snapshot_age) — but introduce staleness tension with novelty/dedup's need for mutable union-set membership

github.com/risingwavelabs/risingwave.name: risingwavelabs/risingwave
github.com/risingwavelabs/risingwave.what: Streaming DB repositioned for agentic AI: incremental materialized views (only affected results recomputed on change, <100ms freshness), state in cheap object storage, provides MCP server
github.com/risingwavelabs/risingwave.lesson: Define each gauge/novelty-set as an incremental stream-fold (O(delta) not O(total)) so observability cost is scale-independent of iteration count

github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-ru.name: kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime
github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-ru.what: The canonical Kubernetes Reconcile() control-loop library behind every operator; observe→delta→act→requeue-with-backoff, idempotent by contract.
github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-ru.lesson: Model a deep-loop iteration body as a reconciler over an externalized desired-state spec so any crash resumes by re-running Reconcile against last persisted state instead of replaying events; requeue-after is the loop-cadence knob.

github.com/resilience4j/resilience4j.name: resilience4j/resilience4j
github.com/resilience4j/resilience4j.what: Fault-tolerance library: CircuitBreaker (failure-rate hysteresis controller), Bulkhead, RateLimiter, TimeLimiter, Retry, Hedge.
github.com/resilience4j/resilience4j.lesson: CircuitBreaker is a bang-bang feedback controller on failure rate; its Golden Rule (one instance per backend, never shared) implies each fan-out target/sub-agent must own its own failure tracker + bulkhead so one flaky source cannot trip or stall the whole loop.

github.com/robusta-dev/robusta.name: robusta-dev/robusta
github.com/robusta-dev/robusta.what: Kubernetes self-healing/auto-remediation engine: trigger→action playbooks, HolmesGPT AI root-cause, change-tracking correlation.
github.com/robusta-dev/robusta.lesson: Decouple detection policy (when to act) from remediation action (what to do), both declarative; auto-resolve external state when the condition clears — a template for deep-improvement's promote/suppress and for self-correcting loop outputs.

github.com/keephq/keep.name: keephq/keep
github.com/keephq/keep.what: Open-source AIOps/alert platform: YAML workflows (triggers→steps→actions), alert dedup + correlation, LLM enrichment.
github.com/keephq/keep.lesson: Declarative remediation workflows plus AI-driven alert correlation/dedup is a production pattern for novelty-suppression and observability gauges; the dedup+correlation layer is exactly a pre-fan-in reducer.

github.com/dgtlmoon/changedetection.io.name: dgtlmoon/changedetection.io
github.com/dgtlmoon/changedetection.io.what: Poll→diff→alert loop with LLM change-summarization that suppresses irrelevant diffs via plain-English intent rules.
github.com/dgtlmoon/changedetection.io.lesson: Observe→diff-vs-baseline→signal is a loop reduced to its essence; LLM-based intent filtering ('only signal when X') is a concrete instance of novelty metrics driving loop gating and reducing false-positive iterations.

github.com/maximerobeyns/self_improving_.name: MaximeRobeyns/self_improving_coding_agent
github.com/maximerobeyns/self_improving_.what: Coding agent that runs an iterative self-improvement loop on its own codebase, archiving per-iteration benchmark results
github.com/maximerobeyns/self_improving_.lesson: Append-only benchmark archive as the convergence signal source; admits self-improvement is path-dependent/high-variance, so convergence needs multi-seed averaging

github.com/microsoft/skillopt.name: microsoft/SkillOpt
github.com/microsoft/skillopt.what: Text-space optimizer treating skill.md as sole trainable state, with held-out validation gates and bounded add/delete/replace edits
github.com/microsoft/skillopt.lesson: Validation-gate as a principled acceptance/termination criterion (accept edit only if it strictly improves held-out score) plus a bounded mutation vocabulary to make search tractable

github.com/cobusgreyling/loop-engineerin.name: cobusgreyling/loop-engineering
github.com/cobusgreyling/loop-engineerin.what: Practical patterns and CLI starters for designing systems that prompt/orchestrate coding-agent loops
github.com/cobusgreyling/loop-engineerin.lesson: Catalogues the dominant industry loop-control pattern: fixed max-iterations cap + test-pass exit, i.e. the degenerate stop policy deep-loop exists to improve

github.com/hkuds/lightrag.name: HKUDS/LightRAG
github.com/hkuds/lightrag.what: GraphRAG alt (EMNLP'25) with dual-layer KG+vector; incremental KB via local-graph set-merge, no global rebuild
github.com/hkuds/lightrag.lesson: Accumulate per-iteration deltas by local-build-then-set-merge keyed on content, not global re-derivation; add a 'rich-enough' saturation gate that stops enriching a node

github.com/gusye1234/nano-graphrag.name: gusye1234/nano-graphrag
github.com/gusye1234/nano-graphrag.what: Minimal GraphRAG (~1100 LOC) with md5-hash-keyed incremental insert
github.com/gusye1234/nano-graphrag.lesson: Content-hash idempotency is necessary but NOT sufficient: its own caveat shows global re-clustering every insert balloons cost — accumulate idempotently but never globally re-derive structure per iteration

github.com/circlemind-ai/fast-graphrag.name: circlemind-ai/fast-graphrag
github.com/circlemind-ai/fast-graphrag.what: Promptable GraphRAG using personalized PageRank graph exploration (HippoRAG-inspired) with incremental updates + checkpointing notebook
github.com/circlemind-ai/fast-graphrag.lesson: Query the accumulated graph by graph-walk (PageRank seeded by query) rather than flat similarity; wrap every destructive merge in a checkpoint to roll back bad accumulations

github.com/openspg/kag.name: OpenSPG/KAG
github.com/openspg/kag.what: Knowledge-Augmented Generation (v0.8.0 Jun 2025); schema-constrained construction + conceptual semantic reasoning to fix OpenIE noise; static & iterative task-planning
github.com/openspg/kag.lesson: Schema-free entity accumulation degrades; a schema + semantic-alignment/canonicalization step at accumulation time is what keeps the knowledge structure coherent across iterations

github.com/openspg/openspg.name: OpenSPG/openspg
github.com/openspg/openspg.what: Ant Group KG engine; explicit 'continuous iterative evolution of incomplete data states', entity-linking/normalization operators, KGDSL logical reasoning
github.com/openspg/openspg.lesson: Model the loop as iterative evolution over an incomplete-but-typed state: typed schema + normalization operators beat schema-free OpenIE for long-running accumulation

github.com/osu-nlp-group/hipporag.name: OSU-NLP-Group/HippoRAG
github.com/osu-nlp-group/hipporag.what: NeurIPS'24 + ICML'25 'From RAG to Memory'; personalized PageRank + continual knowledge integration across documents
