github.com/osu-nlp-group/hipporag.lesson: Frame cross-iteration accumulation as memory consolidation (continuous integration), and that cheaper indexing can beat heavier GraphRAG on continual-learning — budget and quality are not opposed when the consolidation mechanism is right

github.com/eureka-research/eureka.name: eureka-research/Eureka
github.com/eureka-research/eureka.what: LLM in-context evolutionary optimization over reward code: fan-out N candidates, eval via real RL-environment fitness, reflect, iterate (ICLR 2024).
github.com/eureka-research/eureka.lesson: Make the evaluator environment-execution (ground-truth fitness), not an LLM-judge; expose tiny explicit knobs (sample N, iteration K) and feed fitness back as in-context mutation.

github.com/huggingface/trl.name: huggingface/trl
github.com/huggingface/trl.what: RL post-training loop; GRPOTrainer now supports environment-owned rewards + per-example environment selection across mixed sandboxed suites (v1.8.0, Jul 2026).
github.com/huggingface/trl.lesson: Own the scoring in the evaluated environment, not the loop driver; let multiple evaluator suites coexist in one run so loop control is decoupled from eval semantics.

github.com/openbmb/chatdev.name: OpenBMB/ChatDev
github.com/openbmb/chatdev.what: Virtual software co. (code->test->review) plus Puppeteer (NeurIPS 2025): a learnable RL-optimized orchestrator improving fan-out routing; IER experience lifecycle has explicit elimination operator.
github.com/openbmb/chatdev.lesson: Run a second, slower improvement loop over the fan-out/fan-in policy itself (meta-improvement); give the accumulated experience store an elimination/decay gate, not only dedup.

github.com/open-compass/opencompass.name: open-compass/opencompass
github.com/open-compass/opencompass.what: LLM eval platform; CascadeEvaluator composes sequential evaluator stages, GenericLLMEvaluator makes LLM-judge a first-class stage alongside rule-based (v0.5.3, Jun 2026).
github.com/open-compass/opencompass.lesson: Build deep-review's evaluator as a composable sequential DAG (rule fast-path -> LLM-judge fallback -> human) that can short-circuit per stage for graceful degradation.

github.com/azure-samples/eval-driven-age.name: Azure-Samples/eval-driven-agents
github.com/azure-samples/eval-driven-age.what: Reference implementation of eval-driven development: continuous eval + tracing/telemetry gated through GitHub-Actions CI/CD so every change must clear metrics to merge.
github.com/azure-samples/eval-driven-age.lesson: The merge gate itself is the convergence certificate; map CI/CD-gated eval to runtime/locks-recovery + continuity-threading (admit an iteration only when eval deltas clear threshold with multi-run variance).

github.com/nvidia/garak.name: NVIDIA/garak
github.com/nvidia/garak.what: LLM vulnerability scanner: probe/detector/harness/evaluator/generator plugin architecture (nmap for LLMs)
github.com/nvidia/garak.lesson: Clean 5-role separation of generator-vs-detector-vs-orchestrator-vs-scorer-vs-target; deep-loop conflates these into one 'critic' and that is the evaluator-reliability root cause. Each is an independently swappable plugin with a base class.

github.com/microsoft/pyrit.name: microsoft/PyRIT
github.com/microsoft/pyrit.what: Goal-based multi-turn red-team orchestrator that persists cross-turn conversation memory of which attacks already failed
github.com/microsoft/pyrit.lesson: Externalized-state checkpointing applied to the critique dimension: accumulated memory is 'what attacks failed' = a dedup/novelty signal over the attack/search space, not over document content. v0.14.0 Jun 2026.

github.com/ricommunity/tap.name: RICommunity/TAP
github.com/ricommunity/tap.what: Tree of Attacks with Pruning: branch -> prune-phase-1(off-topic,before target call) -> attack&assess -> prune-phase-2(keep top-k by evaluator score); depth/width/branching params
github.com/ricommunity/tap.lesson: Canonical convergent fan-out/fan-in: prune CHEAP before EXPENSIVE, keep top-k by score, bound all three tree dimensions. Beats PAIR's flat iteration; directly transferable to deep-loop fan-out+convergence+budget as a single parameterized loop.

github.com/centerforaisafety/harmbench.name: centerforaisafety/HarmBench
github.com/centerforaisafety/harmbench.what: Standardized red-team eval pipeline (gen test cases -> merge -> gen completions -> eval) PLUS adversarial_training/ defense half; ships TWO independent judge classifiers (main + held-out validation)
github.com/centerforaisafety/harmbench.lesson: Attack<->defend co-development loop with a held-out validation judge = the 'environment-owned reward' contract made concrete. Trust boundary is 'is the judge itself attackable?', so a second independent judge is mandatory, not optional.

github.com/openai/weak-to-strong.name: openai/weak-to-strong
github.com/openai/weak-to-strong.what: Sandwiching/scalable-oversight loop: weak supervisor labels a stronger model; sweep.py over capability-gap pairs; confidence auxiliary loss
github.com/openai/weak-to-strong.lesson: Naive imitation of a weak critic DEGRADES the stronger model (sycophancy); requires an explicit anti-overconfidence regularizer. Direct warning: deep-ai-council must penalize over-anchoring to the first/weak seat, and capability-in-the-loop is NOT monotonic.

github.com/allenai/reward-bench.name: allenai/reward-bench
github.com/allenai/reward-bench.what: Meta-eval that benchmarks reward models (the critic) themselves; offline RM ensembling; generative-judge ensembles require an ODD count>1 for majority vote; RewardBench 2 Jun 2025
github.com/allenai/reward-bench.lesson: 'Who validates the validator' made operational: ensemble critics with odd seat counts so ties are impossible, and treat any residual tie as 'no convergence' not 'pick one'.

github.com/google/oss-fuzz.name: google/oss-fuzz
github.com/google/oss-fuzz.what: Continuous fuzzing infra (libFuzzer/AFL++/Honggfuzz + ClusterFuzz); regression mode; 13k vulns/50k bugs fixed across 1k projects as of May 2025.
github.com/google/oss-fuzz.lesson: Industrial-grade convergence = coverage-frontier plateau + every fix gated by a regression re-run, not by the fixer's claim; accumulated findings are a test corpus. Direct model for deep-review self-repair-prove-it.

github.com/microsoft/onefuzz.name: microsoft/onefuzz
github.com/microsoft/onefuzz.what: Self-hosted Fuzzing-as-a-Service: programmatic triage, result de-duplication, reproducible crashes, ensemble fuzzing sharing inputs. Archived; reference architecture only.
github.com/microsoft/onefuzz.lesson: Programmatic triage + dedup + guaranteed-reproducible findings is the closed-loop self-repair contract; but an archived project proves you must borrow the architecture, never couple your runtime to one external dependency.

github.com/potsawee/selfcheckgpt.name: potsawee/selfcheckgpt
github.com/potsawee/selfcheckgpt.what: Zero-ground-truth hallucination/confidence detection via cross-sample inconsistency (BERTScore/QA/NLI/LLM-prompt variants).
github.com/potsawee/selfcheckgpt.lesson: A model can't reliably catch its own errors, but cross-sample disagreement is a usable per-claim confidence signal; use it (and an abstaining critic) as a convergence/freshness gauge and human-escalation trigger instead of always-on scoring.

github.com/infer-actively/pymdp.name: infer-actively/pymdp
github.com/infer-actively/pymdp.what: Active inference for MDPs (JAX); Expected Free Energy = instrumental + epistemic value; agents actively seek info then exploit (v1.0.3 Jun 2026, very active).
github.com/infer-actively/pymdp.lesson: Convergence objective should be forward-looking expected information gain about the knowledge graph, not passive novelty plateau; deep-research next-query selection can borrow epistemic-value maximization.

github.com/automerge/automerge.name: automerge/automerge
github.com/automerge/automerge.what: CRDT library with compact history, sync protocol, and tombstones; v3.3.2 Jul 2026 (very active).
github.com/automerge/automerge.lesson: Conflict-free merge + tombstones give formal semantics for concurrent fan-out state AND formal forgetting/elimination of accumulated knowledge — solves determinism-under-concurrency and knowledge-graph decay in one move.

github.com/eleutherai/lm-evaluation-harn.name: EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness
github.com/eleutherai/lm-evaluation-harn.what: Few-shot eval harness backend for the Open LLM Leaderboard; resumable caching, --log_samples, v0.4.x, active Dec 2025.
github.com/eleutherai/lm-evaluation-harn.lesson: Resumable eval caching is RANK/PARALLELISM-dependent — record the parallelism shape in the checkpoint, not just the data; priority-ordered eval procedure + post-hoc sample logging are patterns for deep-improvement eval loops.

github.com/yjs/yjs.name: yjs/yjs
github.com/yjs/yjs.what: CRDT framework: conflict-free shared types, snapshots, undo, tombstone GC; Rust/Python/Go ports
github.com/yjs/yjs.lesson: Prototype the JSONL checkpoint store on a Yjs Y.Map to get lock-free fan-out merge + automatic stale-tombstone forgetting in one subsystem; benchmark vs single-writer replay

github.com/netflix/concurrency-limits.name: Netflix/concurrency-limits
github.com/netflix/concurrency-limits.what: TCP congestion-control (Vegas/Gradient2 AIMD) that auto-detects concurrency limits via latency + Little's Law, with partitioned guarantees
github.com/netflix/concurrency-limits.lesson: Replace static fan-out caps with an AIMD control law: additive-increase on success, multiplicative-decrease on rejection/timeout; use short-vs-long RTT divergence (Gradient2) as an early queuing gauge

github.com/zjunlp/easyedit.name: zjunlp/EasyEdit
github.com/zjunlp/easyedit.what: Knowledge-editing framework with lifelong/continuous editing (WISE, GRACE, UltraEdit), rollback, and formal Reliability/Generalization/Locality/Portability metrics
github.com/zjunlp/easyedit.lesson: Adopt locality+reliability+portability as the per-finding correctness contract for knowledge accumulation; locality is the rigorous form of the regression/novelty gate, and GRACE-style rollback enables reverting one finding without replay

github.com/comp-17/llm-debate-system.name: comp-17/llm-debate-system
github.com/comp-17/llm-debate-system.what: Irving AI-safety-via-debate impl with floor-gated convergence: min 3 rounds, stop after 2 consecutive identical answer-pairs
github.com/comp-17/llm-debate-system.lesson: A cheap, testable debate-convergence rule (floor + N-consecutive-agreement) for deep-ai-council; BUT must be paired with a disagreement/variance gauge because agreement != truth

insights.insight: Use a halt cascade: explicit success or critic approval, semantic-delta plateau with patience, quality or marginal-gain plateau, then unconditional Tmax; implement the cascade once as a pure function so live execution, replay, and post-hoc reasons cannot diverge.

Oscillation detection should operate on normalized action-observation events, not raw text or IDs: exact repeats, repeated errors, agent monologues, and period-2 alternation are distinct gauges with separate thresholds.

Divergent research should avoid pruning by a single scalar too early: retain a Pareto frontier, use controlled exploration and merge, and define saturation over frontier novelty or hypervolume rather than only best-score improvement.

Make fan-in budget-aware: if candidates or paths agree, skip redundant branches; if they disagree or confidence is low, expand the branch set or invoke the expensive judge. This is conditional widening, not fixed fan-out.
A loop is safe only when a bound covers the entire repeated feedback path, including retries, handoffs, tool re-entry, and state growth; a max-iterations setting elsewhere is not sufficient.

Make the JSONL stream the canonical fact log and derive current state, gauges, continuity, and novelty indexes as deterministic projections; compaction must preserve parent sequence and projection hashes.
Encode replay semantics per event as reuse, pure re-execution, or compensation/reconciliation; a checkpoint without a side-effect receipt is not a safe resume boundary.
Give each logical iteration a stable idempotency key and each attempt a monotonic sequence or epoch; commit fan-out results independently and let fan-in consume only durable child completions.
Treat replay compatibility as a versioned contract: record code, graph, model, provider, tool, prompt, and schema fingerprints, then reject, migrate, or fork runs when the contract changes.

Recovery verification should fault-inject append, tool-return, checkpoint, and fan-in boundaries, then compare canonical projection hashes and external-effect receipts; replay correctness needs an operational test harness.

Fan-in should close on an explicit barrier and pass a branch-labelled outcome set to a reducer; the reducer may be deterministic, associative, or model-based, while branch errors remain visible as data.
Deterministic branch scheduling does not imply deterministic result order; store logical branch IDs and canonical indexes, and record arrival order separately.

Partial-failure tolerance must be an explicit fan-in policy such as strict, quorum, deadline, or progressive; retries need per-error allowlists and must not silently convert failed branches into missing data.

Safe parallel reduction requires an algebraic contract such as associativity and commutativity, plus idempotence when duplicate delivery is possible; otherwise reduce in stable order and model late results explicitly.
A model reducer can consume all branch outputs across multiple layers, but fan-out width and layer depth should be controlled by marginal novelty per token rather than fixed branch counts.
Treat evaluator optimization as a typed state machine: propose, score, update, full-validate, accept or reject.
Keep raw judge and step-level scores before reduction so disagreement, rescoring, and evaluator drift remain observable.
External evidence and process-level checks are stronger repair signals than final-score-only self-critique.
Adversarial verification should adapt attacks by vulnerability coverage and marginal novelty, not run a fixed prompt list.
Self-consistency needs an effective-independence gauge: many sampled paths or judges can share correlated errors.
Reflection should produce a typed, scoped memory candidate and require an explicit promotion decision; raw traces, reflective summaries, and accepted lessons should not share the same trust level.
Separate reflector and curator roles, then apply incremental deltas instead of rewriting the whole memory; attach helpful/harmful evidence and deduplication metadata to each lesson.
Tool observations should be hard evidence boundaries in the loop state, allowing reflection to distinguish failed execution, bad assumptions, and unsupported reasoning.
Tree and graph reasoning require path-aware convergence: branch value, parent hash, evaluator evidence, merge provenance, and branch cost must survive fan-in.
Self-refinement is not monotonic by default: feedback quality, memory selection, context growth, and evaluator calibration determine whether later iterations improve or merely preserve errors.
Effective judge count should be estimated from behavioral independence and marginal utility, not the number of seats.
Anonymous peer review should be persisted separately from model identity, then passed to a chairman or adjudicator as a blind evidence bundle.
Seat diversity needs incentive and role-fidelity tests; cooperation, competition, and malicious behavior are distinct deliberation regimes.
Consensus is not a sufficient adjudication signal: debate can amplify position, verbosity, chain-of-thought, and bandwagon biases, while meta-judge protocols may resist them differently.
Debate termination should use stability of verdict distributions and unresolved minority evidence, not fixed rounds or raw agreement.

Cluster semantically equivalent branches before search control, then apply probability and exploration pressure at the cluster level; this removes paraphrase waste without suppressing genuinely different directions.

Maintain an online coverage ledger with semantic topics, unsatisfied subgoals, and periodic reclustering; create a new topic when similarity falls below a threshold, but periodically rebuild clusters to control drift.
Persistent novelty signals such as code-coverage deltas can supervise both memory formation and exploration, distinguishing exhausted behavior from genuinely unseen behavior.

Cross-task memory should be judged by conditional transfer: memory can help difficult or context-insufficient tasks while harming easy tasks, and knowledge memory does not automatically transfer to execution memory.
Global coverage needs its own controller: similarity retrieval finds nearby evidence, while hierarchical global selection searches for corpus-level themes and missing regions.
Budget accounting should track request count, input tokens, output tokens, tool calls, dollars, and wall time at parent, agent, session, and branch scope.
Use a global budget price or dual variable to allocate extra depth to difficult or uncertain tasks and target an average spend, rather than assigning equal rounds to every task.
As the remaining budget shrinks, shift search from broad fan-out toward greedy exploitation and prune branches using residual progress rather than absolute self-confidence.
Early exit should be driven by marginal quality per marginal cost and should persist the best-so-far answer plus residual budget for continuation.
Concurrent delegation needs atomic reservation or non-duplicable budget ownership; post-hoc counters cannot reliably prevent overspend races.
[runtime/gauges-observability] Model duration-bearing work as spans, progress and state changes as timestamped events, and convergence curves as derived metrics.

[runtime/gauges-observability] A versioned telemetry vocabulary is necessary because GenAI conventions are actively evolving, including agent span boundaries, tool attributes, evaluation events, and streaming metrics.
[runtime/state-jsonl-checkpointing] Reproducible traces need typed lineage, timestamps, model/tool configuration, privacy-aware payload identity, and evaluator records—not only final text output.
[runtime/gauges-observability] Immutable execution records plus late-arriving scores form a clean append-only pattern for evaluator and review loops.
[runtime/convergence] Useful dashboards need multiple resolutions: run health, experiment deltas, and trace/node/path/step progression.
Represent coverage as weighted necessary query or claim slots, separating characteristic discovery queries from extractive retrieval queries; final-answer scores can hide missing hops.
Use an anytime marginal-gain scheduler: broaden early, deepen promising branches, prune redundant paths, and reallocate budget during execution.
Define diversity over perspectives, source families, and stances, with a source-independence penalty; lexical novelty alone is not a reliable diversity signal.
Make retrieval granularity a first-class action and charge every granularity transition for tokens, latency, and redundant reads.
Track evidence acquisition separately from report quality using supported-claim coverage, effective citation count, and citation accuracy.
Replay should inject results by stable logical action identity and execute only after the first missing committed receipt; loop control must be deterministic over recorded outputs.
Use separate logical_action_id, attempt_id, and external idempotency key; retries share the logical key while each concrete attempt remains observable.
A JSONL checkpoint is not an exactly-once side-effect guarantee; non-idempotent effects need an atomic receipt/transaction boundary or an explicit compensation path.
Recovery ownership needs fencing: attach a monotonically increasing lease epoch to execution records and reject late appends from superseded workers.
Persist waits as first-class durable events, release worker capacity while waiting, then replay to the wait checkpoint when time or an external signal satisfies it.

A recoverable checkpoint needs both committed state and an execution receipt layer: ordinary completed-task writes may be reapplied, while error, interrupt, resume, and missing-write tasks must be rerun.
Deterministic fan-in must be encoded in replay: sort child task paths and write indices using stable identifiers before applying reducers.
Replay and fork require explicit JSONL modes and immutable lineage: replay reruns a checkpoint suffix, while fork creates a new child branch without rewriting the source history.

An interrupt is a deterministic continuation record keyed by thread, namespace, task, interrupt ID, and resume position; node restart semantics make pre-interrupt effects require idempotency or separate effect nodes.
Delta storage needs a compaction invariant: retain a full seed snapshot plus every on-path delta or rebase the branch before pruning ancestors.
Maintain separate Task Ledger and Progress Ledger state: one records remaining work, the other records whether recent worker actions are making progress; revise the plan only after stagnation.
Make dispatch mode explicit: adaptive selector for open-ended exploration, deterministic graph for required ordering, conditional branches, and cycles.
Represent fan-in with named activation groups and explicit all/any semantics; an any-join must record the winning branch and disposition of late siblings.
Persist typed handoff metadata instead of inferring transitions from prose: target type, condition source, after-work fallback, and parent pattern.
Use composable termination objects evaluated on message deltas, while keeping CrewAI-style guardrail retries local to the worker and separate from global convergence counts.
A textual gradient should be a structured, path-sensitive feedback artifact—not a scalar score; aggregate critiques from every downstream use before updating a target.
Separate cheap proposal feedback from expensive acceptance: minibatch or trace feedback generates candidates, while a held-out validation loop promotes the incumbent only on improvement.

Optimizer strategy should be an adapter over one candidate ledger: textual-gradient descent, Bayesian search, coordinate ascent, history-conditioned generation, and staged critique/synthesis can share parent IDs, metrics, budgets, and promotion rules.
Execution traces and exception nodes are optimization signals: persist them before candidate promotion so recovery and replay can reuse failures as targeted feedback.
For label-poor settings, pairwise preference plus Double Thompson Sampling can replace absolute scoring, but the loop must track comparison uncertainty, judge noise, partial labels, and regret.
Implement stuck detection as semantic signatures over action-observation-error windows, with separate counters for repetition, repeated failure, monologue, alternation, and context errors.
Use a typed retry hierarchy: provider retry, parser/action requery, same-task step retry, fresh attempt, and multi-attempt chooser; each needs its own retry count and cost budget.

Append a durable step record before retry or attempt promotion, including environment state, model statistics, error class, and terminal reason; this makes interrupted runs replayable and unattended limit exits safe.
Make planning checkpoints, per-step callbacks, replay, and final-answer validation part of the loop contract rather than optional logging.
Benchmark ACI surface and executor backend as independent control variables: tool choice changes path cost, while executor abstraction determines session durability and fan-out behavior.
Use two verifier planes: dense per-sample success scores for promotion, plus sparse transcript scanners for reward hacking, evaluator awareness, refusals, and environment faults.
Make grader failure a third state—indeterminate/error—with retry or quarantine semantics; never silently convert parser, transport, or execution failures into score zero.
Promote semantic outcome equivalence over reference-path similarity: equivalent database or environment postconditions should deduplicate candidates even when tool sequences differ.
An agentic grader should inspect artifacts and tool evidence in a controlled read-only workspace, while preserving its own tool calls and metadata as part of the verdict.

Candidate selection should be staged: deterministic contract checks, then rubric or trajectory grading, then multi-objective aggregation; expensive judges should not run on candidates that already fail hard gates.

Model branch selection as fixed-confidence best-arm identification: allocate work toward competitive challengers and stop only when a time-uniform likelihood or confidence boundary separates the leader.
For asynchronous fan-in, certify that unfinished branches cannot overturn the current result by bounding future vote movement; record the certificate as a margin against a full-budget counterfactual.
Use two calibrated stopping risks: premature confidence and continued work without sufficient progress. A lower progress boundary is needed for hard or unsolvable instances.

Sequential stopping should calibrate false-stop risk from trace data and preserve alpha, calibration split, power, and shift diagnostics; SW-UCB threshold adaptation is useful but is not itself a correctness certificate.

Formalize diminishing returns as maximum expected marginal utility per cost, optionally backed by adaptive-submodularity or weak-submodularity tests; stop when no available action clears the reservation value.
Continuation should have a measurable wrong-turn hazard: multi-turn execution can be worse than a fresh single-shot run because early assumptions become anchors.
Artifact-preserving loops need reversible round-trip tests and semantic integrity gates; final-answer quality is insufficient when intermediate state mutates.
Correction capability and mistake-detection capability are separate; correction should be blocked when the verifier cannot localize an error with calibrated confidence.

Extra depth has a critical point: after initial gains, longer reasoning can increase variance and reduce accuracy, so fixed budgets should favor independent short paths over one indefinitely extended path.
Loop admission should be conditional rather than universal: correctness predictors or key-condition checks can identify when intervention is useful and when preserving the baseline is safer.
Synthetic reformulations need lineage fields such as parent_query_id, transform_type, synthetic_text, and grounded_source_ids; synthetic text should contribute zero support until verified.
Novelty should be a vector, separating semantic/document novelty from independent-source and new-claim novelty; embedding distance alone can reward correlated or near-duplicate sources.
Use baseline-first adaptive branch admission: spawn HyDE, paraphrase, or fusion branches only when ambiguity, weak lexical match, low coverage, or low source diversity justifies their cost.
Make the relevance/diversity weight query-adaptive and quality-conditioned; Vendi-RAG adjusts it from answer quality, while DF-RAG searches the diversity parameter with planner/evaluator feedback.

Add explicit over-search, under-search, marginal-claim-gain, source-independence, and contradiction gauges; query diversity is difficult to characterize and search decisions correlate with uncertainty.
Make memory commits transactional: append immutable evidence events first, then publish a derived vector/graph snapshot identified by snapshot_id and digest in the continuity record.
Use dual-lane memory formation: hot-path writes only for high-salience facts, with background fan-in for extraction, consolidation, and pattern discovery.
Seed retrieval with semantic or lexical top-k, then expand through provenance-carrying temporal and relational edges; vector-only retrieval is weak on disconnected multi-hop evidence.
Treat compaction as a reversible derivation, not deletion: every compressed note should retain source event IDs, discarded-span statistics, and supersession links.

Add memory-specific loop gates: replay the same injected wrong assumption under continuation, fresh restart, and branch rollback, scoring stale-memory use, correction latency, forgetting, evidence availability, and cost.
Reducers should be typed and field-specific, with explicit merge algebra and branch-local state snapshots.
Hierarchical reducers need a bounded collapse stage before final synthesis; token limits and retry caps are part of reducer correctness, not merely prompt optimization.
Candidate selection and generative synthesis should be separate nodes so weak or redundant branches do not dominate the final merge.
Carry an immutable task anchor through every hierarchy level; without skip-connections, intermediate reducers can dilute or distort the original objective.
Conflict resolution should emit a structured conflict object before choosing a winner, because LLM judges can select evidence without justification or rely on prior beliefs.
Exactly-once is boundary-specific, not a universal agent property: at-most-once per retry still permits later workflow retries, while external effects need idempotency or compensation.
Generate idempotency keys inside a persisted step and reuse them across retries; deriving them outside the checkpoint boundary can change the key during replay and duplicate side effects.
Compensation should be a durable recovery graph with reverse-order or dependency-aware actions, not an implicit rollback flag.
Lock recovery needs shared leases, fencing, namespace-qualified keys, fairness, and observable queue state; local process locks are insufficient for multi-controller loops.
Parallel resume must be branch-aware: resuming one suspended branch cannot replace sibling state, suspend payloads, or remaining retry budgets.
A human interruption must be a durable state transition containing pending decisions, target action, actor, expiry, and resume context; appending a message to the transcript is insufficient.
An edit must supersede persisted intent and rebase downstream work; changing only executed tool arguments can leave the original tool call eligible for regeneration.
Steering should be an ordered, scoped event stream with explicit effective boundaries, rather than an untyped prompt injection.
An editable plan-as-document can identify step ownership and dependencies, then invalidate and recompute only downstream work after a human edit.
Interruption evaluation should score intent adoption, stale-action rate, rollback or compensation, recovery latency, and token replay against repeated-attempt baselines.
Use a Lagrange price for compute: continue only when estimated quality gain exceeds lambda times marginal cost, and tune lambda by monotone feedback or binary search to hit the run-wide budget.
Make model tier, branch count, and reasoning depth a joint action space; cheap multi-sampling can dominate one expensive call for some difficulty regions.
Allocate hierarchically: reserve a task envelope, assign local budgets from subquestion uncertainty, and return unused capacity to the fan-in scheduler.
A useful stop gauge is posterior state stability, not raw confidence: sequential agreement can stop sampling, while bandit-controlled thresholds adapt to difficulty.
Compute payoff is non-monotone; every loop mode needs an overthinking gauge and an explicit shorten, stop, or revise action.
Use stable rollout IDs for semantic work units and child attempt IDs for retries; aggregate status upward while charging and diagnosing at both levels.
Add a compile-and-lock boundary between loop intent and execution, with static validation, capability allowlists, sanitized outputs, and pre-write threat checks.
Branch-aware continuation needs side-effect lineage, inverse operations, and explicit rebase or invalidation semantics; state snapshots alone are insufficient.
A portable loop contract should expose observation, action, reward, termination, and truncation separately, enabling replay and comparable trajectory metrics.
Alignment evaluation should use a Pareto or veto model over utility and security rather than collapsing both into one scalar reward.
Split convergence into branch termination and parent-frontier termination; a stable branch becomes a terminal candidate while the parent may still widen.
Replace binary CONTINUE/STOP allocation with STOP_BRANCH, DEEPEN, WIDEN, and PROMOTE actions chosen from feedback and marginal value.
Depth admission and convergence are different controls: route easy inputs to short execution before starting a deep loop, then apply trajectory-aware stopping only to admitted tasks.
Add selection regret as a gauge: candidate or oracle coverage minus realized quality of the selected output; stop broadening when added coverage is no longer selectable.
Use multi-fidelity fan-out: allocate cheap probes broadly, promote promising and behaviorally distinct branches, and retain occasional full-depth sentinels to measure false-prune risk.

Give every stage a replay class: DETERMINATE for identical ordered output, UNORDERED for an identical multiset, and INDETERMINATE for potentially different values. Persist an ordered hash plus a hash of sorted content-hash/count pairs.

Allocate branch_id, sample_id, and attempt_id before dispatch; record arrival_seq only as telemetry. Content-address deterministic work, but include sample identity for independent stochastic rollouts and fail closed when inputs cannot be canonically tokenized.
insights.evidence: Dask client, as_completed, and tokenization docs: https://distributed.dask.org/en/stable/client.html ; https://distributed.dask.org/en/stable/api.html ; https://docs.dask.org/en/latest/generated/dask.tokenize.tokenize.html

insights.insight: Model novelty exclusion as claim, commit, and release events carrying dedup_key, generation, owner_event_id, and expiry. Compare owner and generation on every release or commit so an expired worker cannot overwrite a renewed claim.
insights.evidence: Prefect cache isolation and Asynq owner-checked Lua transitions: https://docs.prefect.io/v3/concepts/caching ; https://github.com/hibiken/asynq/blob/master/internal/rdb/rdb.go

insights.insight: Split fan-in into monotonic collection and coordinated finalization. Results may append concurrently, but semantic dedup retractions, top-k, all-complete, convergence, and stopping execute only after a versioned epoch-close barrier.
insights.evidence: CALM theorem and Prefect concurrent-cache behavior: https://arxiv.org/abs/1901.01930 ; https://docs.prefect.io/v3/concepts/caching

insights.insight: Define a canonical reduction tree over sorted branch IDs, quantize evaluator scores before aggregation, and use stable tie-break tuples. Fuzz permutations, worker counts, tree shapes, and duplicate injection around every convergence threshold.

Make order invariance a convergence prerequisite: run paired A/B and B/A evaluations, map both verdicts into one canonical frame, and convert disagreement into abstention or escalation rather than a synthetic winner.
insights.evidence: FairEval: https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.17926; systematic mitigation audit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.23178

insights.insight: Maintain two verbosity channels: observed preference and a length-controlled counterfactual, supplemented by filler-expansion and completeness-truncation controls; there is no reliable universal length penalty.
insights.evidence: Length-Controlled AlpacaEval: https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.04475; cross-family audit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.23178

insights.insight: Add a self-preference firewall: blind explicit provenance, prohibit judge-authored rewrites of candidates, rotate generator and judge families, and log each judge's own-family residual plus leave-one-family-out winner stability.

Council diversity and aggregation robustness are separate controls: obtain independent pre-votes from disjoint model families, estimate vote correlation and leave-one-out influence, then use robust aggregation or abstention rather than an arithmetic mean.

For verifiable tasks, de-anchor the judge by requiring an independent committed solution before revealing the candidate; combine this with hidden anchors and adversarial canaries, retaining every intermediate judgment for replay.
insights.evidence: RobustJudge: https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.09443; commit-first self-play audit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.05904

insights.insight: Replace free-form newInfoRatio with claim-level redundancy-discounted gain: assign each evidence item to versioned aspects and compute gain using the alpha-nDCG factor (1-alpha)^prior_hits. Track corroboration separately as confidence gain, never as coverage gain.

Make STOP versus PIVOT a two-dimensional policy: low marginal novelty plus low estimated unseen mass permits STOP; low marginal novelty plus high unseen mass forces a pivot; high marginal novelty continues the current lane.

Generate several pivot candidates, then choose the one with maximum quality-weighted submodular marginal gain against the accumulated coverage set. Log the winning gain and rejected candidates so pivot choice is replayable.

For concurrent broadening, construct a similarity kernel over candidate branches and select a k-DPP batch; persist the kernel version, selected determinant or log-determinant, and candidate IDs to make diversity decisions reproducible.

Represent every nondeterministic call with separate scheduled, completed, failed, and indeterminate JSONL events; replay may consume a completed result but must never silently regenerate the same attempt.

Derive event_id from run_id, writer_epoch, step_id, attempt_id, and event_kind, and append only when expected_seq matches; this closes duplicate-append and competing-writer races but not the remote-call crash gap.
Lease expiry should trigger takeover, while a sink-enforced writer_epoch provides safety; late responses from older epochs should increment an orphan-result gauge and remain non-canonical.
Record seed, backend fingerprint, hardware, batching, and precision for diagnosis, but treat the immutable observed output as the only replayable model result.

Pin driver_version, policy_version, and event_schema_version at run creation; changed durable-call topology requires an explicit compatibility branch, migration, or new run rather than replay under the new code.

Reflection eligibility should depend on feedback provenance: intrinsic, peer, tool, environment, human, and oracle feedback must not be pooled. Preserve the original candidate and require verified improvement before promotion.

Use a transition ledger and continue only when P(initial-wrong)*P(W→C) exceeds P(initial-correct)*P(C→W) plus marginal token and tool cost. On MATH, prompted correction changed 65.4% to 51.4% with 15.4% C→W; trained IFT+DPO changed 72.8% to 78.6% with 6.0% W→C and 0.2% C→W.

Error localization is the bottleneck, not necessarily repair: best exact localization was 52.87%, while supplying the true first-error location enabled substantial backtracking gains. Route unlocalized cases to independent branches rather than destructive refinement.

Failed trajectories are active contaminants: contextual drag caused 10–20% drops across 11 models and eight tasks, surviving external feedback and successful self-verification. Store rejected traces for audit, but exclude them from the next model context unless distilled into evidence-bound failure facts.

Reflective memory needs admission control. A 2026 study found 16 frozen ALFWorld environments where none of 121 reflections named the correct target; programmatic failure extraction raised correct mentions from 0% to 86% and reduced reflection repetition from 0.64 to 0.10.

Adopt vote-first, debate-by-exception: collect independent answers, aggregate them, then open debate only for split votes, verifier conflicts, high-impact uncertainty, or credible minority objections.

Route debate conditionally by task difficulty, base-model strength, and risk domain: its marginal value rises on harder tasks and weaker models, while collaborative safety discussion can amplify attacks unless panel diversity is deliberately introduced.

Replace agreement-only convergence with a progress-and-drift gate measuring claim-level novelty, relevance to the original question, feedback quality, unresolved dissent, and answer correctness where verification is available.

Adjudicators should receive independent first answers, cited evidence, historical seat reliability, and an explicit dissent register; self-reported confidence and final consensus should remain non-binding features.

Use diversity-aware transcript compression: retain arguments farthest from both one another and the majority, plus evidence-bearing corrections, instead of replaying the complete debate to every seat.
Expose four replay modes—state-only audit, recorded-effect replay, pinned live re-execution, and exact-prefix/live-suffix fork—because each provides a different guarantee and cost.

Derive a per-effect seed from a root seed and stable causal event ID, and record dispatch plus reducer merge order; a global RNG seed can drift when concurrent branches consume randomness in different orders.

Keep large prompts, responses, and tool results in encrypted content-addressed sidecars while JSONL stores hashes and references; this preserves auditability without making replay logs unbounded or duplicative.

Add replay gauges for requested versus achieved fidelity, first divergent event, external calls avoided, replay token cost, storage bytes, and deterministic-inference slowdown; reproducibility consumes both throughput and retained data.
Separate exact trace replay from structured-experience replay: summarized experiences can cheaply guide future loops but cannot reproduce the original causal execution.

Make loop length endogenous: continue branch j only while an upper confidence bound on expected verified quality gain exceeds a budget shadow price times predicted incremental cost; retain maxIterations as a hard safety ceiling.
Implement competing terminal states, stop-success and stop-futility, with thresholds calibrated to an explicit premature-stop risk on historical JSONL replays rather than hand-tuned novelty cutoffs.
For council fan-in, maintain posterior stability of the leading semantic hypotheses and stop when the probability that further samples change the winner falls below the cost-sensitive threshold.

Assign each unexplored research branch a reservation value derived from its payoff distribution and inspection cost; explore the highest value first and stop when the incumbent dominates all remaining branches.

For multi-lineage execution, replace equal fixed depths with ASHA-style budgets: grace iterations, comparable evaluation rungs, promotion of high-value lineages, and early pruning of low-value lineages.

Create a council attack matrix with paired honest and attack runs: vary malicious-seat count, majority control, shared-model family, seat order, transcript exposure, and injected evidence while holding questions, token budgets, and adjudicators fixed.

Promote speculative replay segments only through layered verification: verify candidate tokens under a fixed inference schedule, then verify tool-call equality and the resulting state root; record an explicit exactPrefixEnd before any virtualized live suffix begins.

Split replay integrity from payload retention: hash-chain opaque event envelopes and ciphertext digests, keep each sensitive payload under a separate data-encryption key, and represent erasure as key destruction plus an authenticated redaction certificate.

An anytime-valid stopping gauge must count evidence units compatible with its martingale assumptions; correlated outputs from one model family should be clustered or modeled conditionally instead of being treated as independent council votes.

Extend each JSONL decision record with state features, available actions, chosen stop-or-continue action, behavior-policy probability, incremental cost, delayed reward, terminal cause, and censoring; otherwise historical traces cannot support defensible doubly robust policy comparison.

Learnability reward: score each iteration by whether its findings improve downstream task performance (pass-rate delta), not by correctness or novelty alone. Iterations that produce correct-but-non-transferable findings get zero reward.
insights.evidence: LeapLabTHU/Absolute-Zero-Reasoner PROPOSE→SOLVE loop with learnability reward (arxiv 2505.03335)

insights.insight: Template collapse detection: decompose novelty into within-iteration diversity (conditional entropy H(Z|X)) and cross-iteration distinguishability (mutual information I(X;Z)). High entropy + low MI = the loop is generating diverse-looking but input-agnostic templates — invisible to entropy-only gauges.

SNR-adaptive budget filtering: use reward-variance as a lightweight proxy to skip low-signal iterations before committing full compute. Zero-variance iterations (all rollouts agree) carry no learning signal and should be short-circuited.
insights.evidence: mll-lab-nu/RAGEN SNR-Adaptive Filtering (top-p and top-k modes)

insights.insight: Bounded off-policy window for async fan-out: allow exactly N steps of staleness between when a parallel iteration starts and when its results are merged, with importance-sampling correction weighting stale results. Beyond the threshold, discard rather than merge.
insights.evidence: verl-project/verl one_step_off_policy + transfer_queue; OpenRLHF TIS/ICEPOP IS correction thresholds

insights.insight: Nested group credit assignment: for multi-step agent loops, compute advantages at two levels — episode-level (did the whole iteration succeed?) and step-level (was this particular sub-action better than alternatives at the same state?). This enables fine-grained quality scoring without a critic.

Reframe recovery as 'partition backfill' over a (iteration, branch, step) coordinate grid: a crashed/stale branch re-executes in place while completed sibling artifacts are read by reference, never recomputed. Current deep-loop recovery replays too coarsely.

Adopt named per-resource concurrency pools (Airflow Pools / Dagster concurrency keys) so budget control and lock-recovery share ONE mechanism: each cost center (gpt-5, websearch, a critic-model) gets a named semaphore the scheduler enforces, instead of scattered rate-limit logic.

A waiting fan-in reducer must release its slot between checks (reschedule semantics); a polling gate that holds its token/budget while waiting on the slowest branch is a documented deadlock pattern. deep-loop's fan-in gate should be a sensor, not a busy waiter.
insights.evidence: apache/airflow sensor mode=poke deadlock footgun resolved by mode=reschedule

insights.insight: Field-tested DAG engines treat indefinite retry as an anti-pattern (max_retries=3 then halt-for-intervention), contradicting agentic-loop optimism. deep-loop should pair each retryable branch with a max-retry cap and escalate to a backfill/abort decision rather than silently looping.

Make fan-out width a runtime, pool-bounded decision whose branches emit content-addressed artifacts; the control plane (not the loop body) owns scheduling, retries, and merging. This decouples 'how many parallel research branches' from the loop logic itself.

Address fan-out workers by stable identity (Orleans grain key) rather than as anonymous ephemeral tasks: a re-visit of the same angle in a later iteration re-activates the same logical worker with accumulated context, a crash transparently re-activates elsewhere from last persisted state, and idle workers free memory automatically — fusing fan-out and checkpointing
insights.evidence: Orleans grain identity + auto-activation/deactivation + WriteStateAsync persistence model

insights.insight: Use Chandy-Lamport checkpoint-barrier injection for consistent-cut snapshots across concurrent fan-out: emit a barrier into the event stream, each sub-agent snapshots partial state on receipt, the iteration is durably checkpointed only when all barriers are collected — but recognize the coordination overhead is net-negative for small fan-outs (3-8 agents)
insights.evidence: Flink async barrier snapshotting in flink-state-backends/flink-runtime

insights.insight: Make the checkpoint-advance atomic: state-append + output-emit + cursor-advance as one transactional unit (Kafka Streams consume-produce-commit pattern), preventing double-processing on retry
insights.evidence: Kafka Streams exactly-once via transactional offset+output commit

insights.insight: Add periodic snapshots to the JSONL journal so resume replay is O(snapshot_age) not O(iterations): but resolve the explicit tradeoff between fast mutable novelty-set checks (snapshot + mutable set) and audit-pure replay (O(N) rebuild) — this is a real design fork for dedup-novelty, not a free win
insights.evidence: Pekko Persistence snapshot cadence vs full-event-replay recovery

insights.insight: Model gauges (novelty, convergence, budget) as incremental materialized views over the event log: a novelty seen-set as an incremental union (O(1) membership), convergence as incremental EWMA over per-iteration deltas — making observability cost scale-independent of iteration count rather than O(total) recompute per iteration
insights.evidence: RisingWave incremental computation: only affected MV results recomputed on delta

insights.insight: Reconcile() over desired-state, not event replay, is the resilient control-loop primitive: the loop body is idempotent and recovers from any crash by re-deriving the action from current actual vs. persisted desired — eliminating the need to replay a fragile event log.

A circuit breaker is feedback control on failure rate with hysteresis (CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN); the production discipline is per-target isolation so the control action for one failing source never contaminates the others — map this to per-sub-agent failure trackers in fan-out.
insights.evidence: resilience4j/resilience4j CircuitBreaker + Golden Rule (instance-aware patterns must not share)

insights.insight: Self-correction can be formalized as a dynamical system with error dynamics and stability conditions — convergence of iterative refinement is a control-theoretic stability question, giving a principled basis for termination/stopping rules rather than ad-hoc delta thresholds.
insights.evidence: arXiv:2604.22273 'Self-Correction as Feedback Control: Error Dynamics, Stability' (2026, abstract verified via search)

insights.insight: Under distress the control-theoretically correct move is often to SUSPEND the feedback loop (open circuit / fail fast) rather than tighten it — this directly tensions deep-loop's default 'keep converging' assumption and operationalizes the when-NOT-to-loop thread.
insights.evidence: resilience4j open-circuit fail-fast philosophy vs classical PID stability goal

insights.insight: Separating detection policy (when to act) from remediation action (what to do), both declarative, lets the self-healing loop be auditable and re-targetable — Robusta playbooks and Keep YAML are two field-proven shapes deep-improvement's rule layer could adopt.
insights.evidence: robusta-dev/robusta trigger→action playbooks; keephq/keep triggers→steps→actions YAML

insights.insight: Use a held-out validation gate (not the generating trajectory) as the acceptance criterion for each deep-improvement edit: accept only if strictly improves an unseen eval split — this turns 'convergence' from a fixed iteration cap into a monotonic-improvement guarantee and prevents overfitting to in-loop noise

Store an append-only per-iteration benchmark ledger in JSONL state as the convergence signal source and observability gauge; the archive is both the resumability substrate and the input to the next improvement decision

Constrain the mutation/emit vocabulary to a bounded set (add/delete/replace) so the search space stays tractable and convergence remains measurable — unbounded free-form edits make improvement unmeasurable

Most production 2025-2026 'loop engineering' uses a degenerate stop policy (max-iterations cap + test-pass exit); the formal convergence machinery is imported from optimization (validation gates, archives) rather than invented by coding-agent builders
insights.evidence: cobusgreyling/loop-engineering, lushbinary loop guide, 'Ralph Loop' max-iterations pattern

insights.insight: Accumulate per-iteration findings by local-build-then-set-merge into a persistent graph, never re-derive the whole structure per iteration — that's both the continuity mechanism AND the budget control
insights.evidence: HKUDS/LightRAG incremental set-merge; contrasted with nano-graphrag's global re-clustering cost

insights.insight: A 'rich-enough' saturation signal (stop enriching a node once its description is saturated) is a natural, content-driven accumulation/budget stop that replaces arbitrary caps

Hash/similarity dedup is insufficient for accumulation: without an explicit entity-resolution/canonicalization step the graph fragments and novelty scoring silently breaks — typed schema + semantic alignment is the fix
insights.evidence: OpenSPG/KAG 'knowledge alignment using conceptual semantic reasoning to alleviate OpenIE noise'; openspg normalization operators

insights.insight: The next-iteration broadening seed should be a graph-walk (personalized PageRank over accumulated findings) seeded by the current query, not a flat re-rank of the delta pile — graph topology drives coverage

Because accumulation is destructive (merges mutate shared state), every merge must be checkpoint-wrapped for rollback — a bad entity-extraction pass otherwise permanently corrupts the accumulated memory
insights.evidence: circlemind-ai/fast-graphrag checkpointing.ipynb 'to avoid irreversible data corruption'

insights.insight: Evaluator-as-environment-execution vs evaluator-as-LLM-judge: Eureka beats human experts on 83% of tasks using ground-truth RL fitness, not a judge. When judge reliability is suspect, swap in an executable evaluator (run the test, read the metric) as the fitness function for deep-improvement.

Two-layer improvement: ChatDev runs an artifact-level loop (code->test->review) AND a meta-loop (Puppeteer) that RL-optimizes the orchestrator's agent-routing/sequencing. system-deep-loop should improve its own fan-out policy across episodes, not only the artifacts.

IER's experience lifecycle has an explicit ELIMINATION operator (acquisition->utilization->propagation->elimination) — the first concrete mechanism for the open thread on memory decay/pruning. Accumulation needs a forgetting policy, not just dedup.

'eval-driven' is overloaded: EDD means eval-as-GATE (regression prevention, held-out suite) while Eureka means eval-as-FITNESS (search signal, environment reward). deep-review and deep-improvement must use different evaluator contracts; conflating them produces a judge-gated search that neither prevents regressions nor optimizes well.

The 'ralph-loop' (naive bash while-loop re-invoking a coding agent with only max-iterations + test-pass as stop) is now a NAMED community anti-pattern: GitHub's eval-driven-development topic lists it beside quality-gates/plan-lifecycle. The named corrective is measurement-gated admission with multi-run variance, not a smarter stop condition alone.

Prune cheap before expensive, then keep top-k by score: the two-phase pruning fan-out/fan-in (TAP) is the formally parameterized version of what deep-loop's runtime/fan-out-fan-in needs. Phase 1 = off-topic/novelty filter (cheap, pre-target-call); Phase 2 = top-k retention by the evaluator. Both phases are prunable => the loop is convergent by construction, not by luck.

A critique/debate loop needs an explicit anti-sycophancy regularizer, not just more capable participants. Without it, a strong model optimizing a weak critic's score will degrade below the critic's own performance (the weak-to-strong gap goes negative). This is the formal counterexample to 'more capability in the loop monotonically improves'.
insights.evidence: openai/weak-to-strong confidence auxiliary loss; naive-imitation degradation result

insights.insight: Separate 'detector' (checks ONE specific failure mode) from 'evaluator' (aggregates/scores/reports) as distinct plugin interfaces. garak's whole extensibility comes from this seam; deep-loop's single 'critic' role is why evaluator reliability is a flagged failure mode.
insights.evidence: NVIDIA/garak garak/detectors vs garak/evaluators vs garak/harnesses base classes

insights.insight: Adversarial critique loops show diminishing returns FAST (PAIR converges in <20 queries; TAP >80% at small depth), and aggressive fan-out+pruning beats more depth. This contradicts the 'increase depth for better results' heuristic: for safety/robustness discovery, BREADTH-with-pruning dominates DEPTH-with-refinement.

Ship two independent judges and treat disagreement between them as a signal, not noise: HarmBench's main+validation-classifier pair is the cheapest 'is the judge itself compromised' detector. For deep-review/deep-alignment, run the verifier through a second model and gate on agreement.

Council/debate gains are conditional on model-family error-independence; homogeneous peers have correlated blind spots. Make family-diversity mandatory and surface pairwise-disagreement as a novelty gauge, not a vote count.

Treat the accumulated findings set as a regression test corpus: every 'fixed' deep-review finding must survive a re-eval against the whole set (fuzzing regression-mode + bisection), or the fix is unproven.
insights.evidence: google/oss-fuzz regression mode; microsoft/onefuzz reproducible-crash triage

insights.insight: Convergence should be an active info-gain objective (Expected Free Energy: instrumental+epistemic), not a passive novelty plateau — agents should actively probe the knowledge frontier before declaring done.

A single CRDT/tombstone store (automerge) unifies two open threads at once: conflict-free merge for concurrent iteration branches (fan-out determinism) and formal forgetting (tombstones) for the knowledge graph.

Cross-sample self-consistency (SelfCheckGPT) is the practical abstention/confidence signal that makes a critic able to refuse-to-score; abstention-rate then becomes a convergence freshness gauge and human-escalation trigger.

Treat the fan-out window as a TCP congestion window with AIMD control: additive-increase when quality/latency hold, multiplicative-decrease on rejection/timeout, with a delay-divergence early-warning gauge (Gradient2's long-vs-short RTT average). This self-tunes budget under provider load better than any fixed or cost-proportional cap.

Borrow knowledge-editing's four-axis correctness contract — Reliability (fixes target), Generalization (covers paraphrases), Locality (doesn't break unrelated findings), Portability (propagates one hop) — as the gate every accumulated finding/insight must pass before it enters the graph. Locality is the formal version of the 'does this regress prior conclusions?' check.

Rollback-without-replay + conflict-free-merge is achievable by composing GRACE-style per-edit rollback (EasyEdit) with CRDT merge (Yjs): findings become individually-revertable, fan-out-mergeable state rather than an append-only log, paying memory for surgical undo.
insights.evidence: EasyEdit GRACE rollback + UltraEdit lifelong normalization; yjs Y.Map merge semantics

insights.insight: Floor-gated consecutive-agreement (min-rounds floor + 'same verdict N rounds in a row') is a zero-parameter convergence detector for deliberation loops, but it embeds the collusive-failure debate theory warns about; it must be vetoed by a model-family-diversity / pairwise-disagreement variance gauge before stopping.

contradictions.claim: Semantic similarity or stability is sufficient to declare convergence.
contradictions.counter: A loop can converge to a stable but wrong answer; semantic stopping needs quality, critic, execution, or confidence signals.

contradictions.claim: A global iteration or token cap is an adequate termination policy.
contradictions.counter: Caps are progress-blind, and a bound may fail to cover the actual repeated feedback path.

contradictions.claim: More iterations or branches are monotonically better.
contradictions.counter: MATA reports that most refinements finish in one or two rounds, while GEPA-Lite uses non-regression gates because additional mutations can create churn.

contradictions.claim: A checkpoint is enough to prevent duplicate work on resume.
contradictions.counter: Only if it stores completed step outputs or side-effect receipts; LangGraph re-executes post-checkpoint LLM and API calls, while Temporal and Restate reuse recorded history.

contradictions.claim: Exactly-once workflow execution implies exactly-once external effects.
contradictions.counter: Exactly-once usually applies to the durable orchestration layer; external APIs still require idempotency keys, receipts, transactional coupling, or compensation.

contradictions.claim: Persisting a current snapshot is sufficient for long-running loops.
contradictions.counter: Replay also depends on code and schema compatibility; Temporal requires deterministic versioning, and LangGraph warns that positional replay can associate cached results with the wrong call after graph edits.

contradictions.claim: runtime/fan-out-fan-in should always wait for every branch before downstream synthesis.
contradictions.counter: Beam supports early and late emissions, while Ray supports processing ready branches incrementally; an all-branches barrier is only one closure mode.

contradictions.claim: Deterministic parallel execution yields deterministic aggregate output.
contradictions.counter: ADK describes deterministic orchestration but warns result order may be nondeterministic; Beam explicitly sorts unordered grouped values, and Ray actor ordering changes across submitters and retries.

contradictions.claim: Retries are a transparent reliability layer.
contradictions.counter: Ray does not retry application exceptions by default and actor calls can have at-most-once side-effect ambiguity; Beam may invoke user code multiple times, so idempotence or receipts are required.

contradictions.claim: [deep-ai-council/runtime/fan-out-fan-in] More judges should monotonically improve reliability.
contradictions.counter: Nine frontier judges provided only about two effective independent votes; the best single judge sometimes matched or beat the panel.

contradictions.claim: [deep-improvement/deep-review] A fluent natural-language critique is a sufficient repair signal.
contradictions.counter: JETTS found critiques often ineffective, style-focused, or capable of inducing meaningless changes and answer flips; Self-Refine's gains are task-dependent.

contradictions.claim: [deep-review/deep-alignment] A rubric or reference answer makes LLM judging reliable.
contradictions.counter: Rubrics and references help but do not remove long-form instability, position bias, or objective-correctness failures.

contradictions.claim: deep-improvement/runtime/convergence: More reflection iterations should monotonically improve quality.
contradictions.counter: Self-Refine and Reflexion depend on task-specific feedback and memory, while ACE identifies brevity bias and context collapse; every promotion needs held-out regression checks.

contradictions.claim: runtime/continuity-threading: Replaying the full prior trace is the safest cross-iteration memory policy.
contradictions.counter: Reflexion distinguishes full last-attempt replay from compact reflection, while ACE uses structured deltas and counters; full replay increases cost and can preserve repeated mistakes.

contradictions.claim: runtime/budget-cost: Wider tree or graph search is a free reliability improvement.
contradictions.counter: ToT, GoT, and LATS add generation, evaluation, environment, and merge calls; branch factor and reflection frequency must be treated as explicit budget variables.

contradictions.claim: [deep-ai-council, runtime/dedup-novelty] More seats and debate rounds should monotonically improve reliability.
contradictions.counter: Correlated seats can form echo chambers; later debate can amplify shared misconceptions or existing judge bias.

contradictions.claim: [deep-ai-council, deep-alignment, runtime/gauges-observability] Anonymous reviewing is enough to make adjudication unbiased.
contradictions.counter: Anonymity can reduce model-name favoritism, but does not remove order, verbosity, chain-of-thought, or bandwagon effects.

contradictions.claim: [deep-ai-council, runtime/convergence] Majority consensus is a safe stopping signal.
contradictions.counter: Majority pressure can suppress independent correction; a correct minority may require an explicit challenge or sentinel path.

contradictions.claim: runtime/dedup-novelty: low embedding similarity is sufficient evidence of novelty.
contradictions.counter: Semantic equivalence requires entailment or clustering, while useful novelty may be observable only through new coverage, claims, entities, or outcomes.

contradictions.claim: runtime/dedup-novelty: accumulation means never updating or invalidating stored knowledge.
contradictions.counter: Append-only evidence is safe, but derived facts need temporal validity, supersession, and explicit contradiction handling.

contradictions.claim: deep-research: broader search is equivalent to increasing fan-out.
contradictions.counter: Uncontrolled fan-out repeats semantic regions; hierarchical global selection and coverage ledgers are needed to direct expansion toward gaps.

contradictions.claim: runtime/budget-cost: A single parent token cap is sufficient to bound a multi-agent tree.
contradictions.counter: Sub-agent budgets may be isolated, parent limits may not forward, and concurrent shared counters can be best-effort; fan-out requires explicit allocation semantics.

contradictions.claim: runtime/convergence: More test-time reasoning monotonically improves answer quality.
contradictions.counter: Marginal returns diminish, overthinking can abandon correct answers, and optimal depth varies by problem difficulty.

contradictions.claim: runtime/budget-cost: Budget exhaustion should always hard-fail the run.
contradictions.counter: Soft exhaustion with partial output preserves already-paid work, while hard rejection remains appropriate at provider or safety boundaries.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/gauges-observability] A final quality score or aggregate cost curve is sufficient evidence of progress.
contradictions.counter: AgentOps research emphasizes trace inputs, outputs, model data, and checkpoints; Agentic CLEAR adds path, node, and temporal analysis because scalar outcomes hide silent stalls and inefficient trajectories.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/state-jsonl-checkpointing] Re-emitting the same record ID is a safe enrichment mechanism.
contradictions.counter: Langfuse treats traces and observations as immutable; re-ingestion can create duplicates and inflate aggregate metrics, so enrichment should be a separate score or event.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/gauges-observability] Application-level tracing alone provides complete agent observability.
contradictions.counter: AgentSight argues that intent-only and action-only views miss causal linkage; correlating LLM intent with process, file, and kernel effects exposes hidden retries and side effects.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/locks-recovery] GenAI telemetry semantics can be treated as stable once adopted.
contradictions.counter: The official GenAI conventions are still a separate active repository with no releases and frequent schema changes, requiring explicit versioning and compatibility tests.

contradictions.claim: runtime/dedup-novelty: more diverse query families should improve coverage and question answering.
contradictions.counter: Diversity improves retrieval in some settings, but it is difficult to characterize; STORM also reports source-bias transfer and unrelated-fact association risks.

contradictions.claim: deep-research: increasing branch count and depth should improve comprehensiveness.
contradictions.counter: Breadth can improve necessary-query coverage, but excessive retrieval can overflow context and reduce coherent depth; scheduling must optimize coverage per cost.

contradictions.claim: runtime/gauges-observability: citation volume is a useful proxy for research coverage.
contradictions.counter: High effective-citation counts can coexist with weaker citation accuracy, so citation quantity and grounding must be separate gauges.

contradictions.claim: runtime/state-jsonl-checkpointing: a durable checkpoint makes a side effect exactly once.
contradictions.counter: Checkpoint replay prevents re-execution only after the receipt commits; task execution remains at-least-once and can duplicate an effect during the crash window before receipt persistence.

contradictions.claim: runtime/dedup-novelty: one generic idempotency key can cover the whole research run.
contradictions.counter: Inngest event and function deduplication defaults to a 24-hour horizon; long-lived research loops need a retention-aware run ledger aligned with continuation segments.

contradictions.claim: Checkpointing gives a saved place where execution simply resumes.
contradictions.counter: Time-travel replay re-executes the suffix, including external calls and interrupts, so the same checkpoint can produce different outcomes.

contradictions.claim: An interrupt resumes exactly at the suspension line.
contradictions.counter: LangGraph restarts the entire node; code before the interrupt runs again, so side effects must be idempotent.

contradictions.claim: Resume is only a scalar continuation value.
contradictions.counter: Agent Protocol extends correlated input.respond with optional atomic state update and goto routing, creating a wider composite-decision contract.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/continuity-threading] AG2 latest documentation presents classic GroupChat examples as current.
contradictions.counter: The current AG2 README says the v1.0.0b0 protocol-driven package removed classic ConversableAgent/GroupChat and the autogen import; examples must be version-pinned.

contradictions.claim: [deep-improvement/runtime/convergence] Joint end-to-end textual-gradient optimization is a safe default for compound systems.
contradictions.counter: A controlled 2026 study found 49% of runs below zero-shot and no significant prompt interaction in its tested conditions, undermining the assumption that there is always a useful joint gradient.

contradictions.claim: [deep-improvement/runtime/continuity-threading] A prompt that wins validation should transfer across tasks and model backbones.
contradictions.counter: A causal-inspired analysis reports that optimized-prompt superiority often fails to transfer across tasks and LLM backbones; promotion should be scoped to model, task, metric, and dataset version.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/budget-cost/deep-alignment] Ground-truth labels are required for reliable prompt optimization.
contradictions.counter: PDO and self-supervised prompt optimization show that pairwise output preferences can drive label-free search, although judge uncertainty and calibration remain unresolved.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/convergence] Repeated identical actions are sufficient evidence that an agent is stuck.
contradictions.counter: Interactive shells, polling, and long-running commands can repeat actions while executor state is changing; detector thresholds need external progress signals such as file, test, or process deltas.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/budget-cost] More full attempts are a general path to higher success.
contradictions.counter: SWE-agent documents multi-attempt runs as expensive and warns that unbounded iteration makes average cost diverge; mini-SWE-agent favors a simpler bounded trajectory.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/budget-cost] A richer specialized ACI should always improve coding-agent efficiency.
contradictions.counter: The onlycodes study finds tool-surface cost depends jointly on task regime and agent design; pass rates were statistically tied while path costs varied.

contradictions.claim: A reference tool/action trajectory is the correctness oracle.
contradictions.counter: τ-bench normally scores equivalent final database state and communication; partial action matching is diagnostic and can disagree with correctness.

contradictions.claim: A failed grader or missing execution marker should be aggregated as a zero.
contradictions.counter: Inspect Evals treats malformed or missing grader/execution evidence as an infrastructure or sample error so it remains retryable.

contradictions.claim: A stable majority proves the answer is correct.
contradictions.counter: The available certificates concern the modal sampling distribution or agreement with the full-budget vote, not ground-truth correctness.

contradictions.claim: A confidence threshold alone is sufficient for adaptive stopping.
contradictions.counter: Confidence-only rules can spend the full budget on unsolvable cases; a separately calibrated progress or lower-risk boundary is required.

contradictions.claim: Plateau patience is a convergence proof.
contradictions.counter: Patience and percentile pruning are heuristics unless their observations, dependence structure, and error risks are calibrated; unequal-depth branches can make them aggressively misleading.

contradictions.claim: More turns improve reasoning by giving the model more chances to reflect (maps_to: runtime/convergence, deep-research, deep-review).
contradictions.counter: Multi-turn performance can fall 39% versus single-turn because early wrong assumptions are retained rather than repaired.

contradictions.claim: Agentic tools and repeated workflow structure protect long-running artifacts (maps_to: runtime/state-jsonl-checkpointing, runtime/locks-recovery, deep-improvement).
contradictions.counter: DELEGATE-52 reports persistent semantic degradation and no improvement from its agentic tool-use harness.

contradictions.claim: Always-on self-critique is safer than accepting the first answer (maps_to: deep-review, runtime/convergence).
contradictions.counter: Intrinsic self-correction can turn correct answers incorrect; correction becomes reliable only when an error location or falsifiable condition is supplied.

contradictions.claim: Test-time compute scales monotonically with reasoning depth (maps_to: runtime/budget-cost, runtime/convergence).
contradictions.counter: Extended thinking shows a rise-then-fall curve; prolonged reasoning can reduce accuracy and increase uncertainty.

contradictions.claim: A loop should run whenever budget remains (maps_to: runtime/convergence, runtime/gauges-observability).
contradictions.counter: The evidence supports an admission gate: loop only when an independent verifier, key condition, or correctness signal predicts positive value.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/convergence] More query variants and higher retrieval recall should monotonically improve final answer quality.
contradictions.counter: [runtime/budget-cost] Reranking, truncation, redundancy, and latency can neutralize or reverse recall gains; branch admission must be conditional.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/dedup-novelty] Increasing semantic diversity in retrieved documents is sufficient to produce diverse answers.
contradictions.counter: [deep-research] DIVERGE finds standard RAG can repeat viewpoints despite diverse contexts; viewpoint-conditioned generation and historical memory are required.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/gauges-observability] Query embedding clusters are an adequate measure of useful reformulation diversity.
contradictions.counter: [runtime/dedup-novelty] Useful diversity is task- and utility-dependent; simple clustering failed in the cited multi-agent reformulation study.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/continuity-threading] Agent-directed hot-path writes should control memory formation because they maximize immediate learning.
contradictions.counter: [runtime/fan-out-fan-in, runtime/budget-cost] LangMem documents higher latency and tool-choice burden for active writes, while background formation supports deeper extraction without delaying the main response; a dual lane is safer.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/dedup-novelty] More retained notes and graph edges should improve cross-iteration recall.
contradictions.counter: [runtime/budget-cost, runtime/locks-recovery] SimpleMem requires consolidation to control redundancy, AriadneMem distinguishes duplicates from state transitions, and Memora reports obsolete-memory reuse; accumulation without invalidation increases error persistence.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/dedup-novelty] A single embedding index is sufficient for accumulated research memory.
contradictions.counter: [runtime/dedup-novelty, runtime/gauges-observability] HippoRAG and AriadneMem use graph structure for association and bridge discovery, but still rely on semantic or lexical retrieval to seed candidates; the practical design is hybrid and auditable.

contradictions.claim: A generic append or concatenate reducer is sufficient for all branch outputs; maps_to=runtime/dedup-novelty.
contradictions.counter: Branch fields need different operators: append findings, sum errors, preserve histories, and adjudicate conflicts separately.

contradictions.claim: More hierarchy monotonically improves synthesis; maps_to=runtime/convergence.
contradictions.counter: HMAW found a three-layer hierarchy strongest in its study, with deeper configurations degrading performance.

contradictions.claim: A final LLM judge can silently resolve conflicting evidence; maps_to=deep-review.
contradictions.counter: Conflict detection and provenance are needed because models may favor one source without justification.

contradictions.claim: At-most-once execution prevents duplicate side effects end-to-end.
contradictions.counter: At-most-once is defined per retry attempt; a later retry can execute again, and an interrupted external effect may be unknown. Use no-retry, idempotency keys, or compensation.

contradictions.claim: A fixed retry count is sufficient for partial-failure tolerance.
contradictions.counter: Argo and Conductor distinguish transient, terminal, timeout, and conditional failures; some failures should route to recovery or compensation rather than retry.

contradictions.claim: A resumed parallel branch can safely rewrite the complete workflow snapshot.
contradictions.counter: Mastra’s release history documents sibling suspend-state loss during parallel foreach resume, showing that branch-local state needs merge or compare-and-swap semantics.

contradictions.claim: Human-in-the-loop means a final approval button after the loop finishes.
contradictions.counter: The stronger pattern is interruption during execution: edit, retry, escalate, redirect, or change the plan before resuming.

contradictions.claim: An edited tool call is equivalent to replacing the original intent.
contradictions.counter: The original intent must be explicitly superseded; otherwise stale calls may reappear after the edited action.

contradictions.claim: More feedback turns prove that human steering helped.
contradictions.counter: Improvement may come from retries or resampling; evaluation needs an unguided repeated-attempt baseline and a feedback-use metric.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/convergence] More iterations are monotonically beneficial once a solver can continue.
