contradictions.counter: Thinking-Optimal Scaling and early-stopping work report overthinking, accuracy degradation, and domain-specific optimal depth.

contradictions.claim: [runtime/fan-out-fan-in] Majority agreement is a sufficient correctness gauge.
contradictions.counter: Agreement can reflect correlated errors or surface-form mismatch; Adaptive-Consistency explicitly notes that majority matching can fail where self-consistency fails.

contradictions.claim: Restoring externalized state is sufficient for safe rollback.
contradictions.counter: External tool effects may persist after state restoration and require inverse or compensating actions.

contradictions.claim: One aggregate quality score is enough to optimize an agent loop.
contradictions.counter: An agent can complete the user task while violating security constraints, so utility and safety must remain separately gated.

contradictions.claim: Answer stabilization means the research loop has converged.
contradictions.counter: Stabilization can justify stopping one branch, while independent sampling may still uncover rare correct candidates elsewhere.

contradictions.claim: More divergent samples reliably improve the final selected answer.
contradictions.counter: Oracle coverage continued rising, but majority voting and reward-model selection saturated around 100 samples in the reported experiments.

contradictions.claim: Long or full-depth reasoning should be forced whenever quality matters.
contradictions.counter: Thinkless reduced long-reasoning use by 50-90% on its benchmarks, while AB-MCTS found additional refinement valuable on complex feedback-rich tasks; depth must be conditional.

contradictions.claim: Every candidate must run to full depth before safe pruning is possible.
contradictions.counter: DEHB obtains robust anytime and final search performance by using cheap approximate evaluations, successive promotion, and persistent diverse subpopulations.

contradictions.claim: Stable content keys make repeated fan-out submissions idempotent.
contradictions.counter: For stochastic LLM calls, identical inputs may denote intentionally independent samples; pure-function reuse collapses diversity, while noncanonical inputs can generate unstable keys.

contradictions.claim: A matching cache or novelty key prevents duplicate execution.
contradictions.counter: Prefect explicitly permits simultaneous same-key execution under its default cache isolation; exclusion requires serialization or an atomic claim.

contradictions.claim: A TTL uniqueness lock is safe to release when an old task finishes.
contradictions.counter: After expiry and reacquisition, that release can delete the new owner's lock; Asynq avoids this by comparing the stored owner ID before deletion.

contradictions.claim: The same branch values necessarily produce the same aggregate.
contradictions.counter: Parallel split and join topology can change a non-associative result; oneTBB needs a deterministic tree and still documents that its answer may differ from sequential reduction.

contradictions.claim: Position bias is a large, stable directional preference that deep-review can correct with a fixed adjustment.
contradictions.counter: FairEval found severe historical order effects, but a 2026 five-model audit found negligible directional bias while retaining position sensitivity. runtime/convergence should therefore use swapping as an invariance test, not a fixed correction.

contradictions.claim: LLM judges universally prefer longer answers, so deep-ai-council should penalize length.
contradictions.counter: Judge families split among verbosity preference, concision preference, and near-neutrality; longer answers can also be genuinely more complete. deep-review needs judge-specific expansion and truncation controls.

contradictions.claim: Removing authorship labels or normalizing style eliminates self-preference in deep-ai-council.
contradictions.counter: Judges recognize their own generations without labels, and self-preference can recover after stronger stylistic neutralization. deep-alignment must measure perturbation invariance and own-family residuals rather than assuming anonymity worked.

contradictions.claim: A detailed rubric or a larger jury is monotonic reliability insurance.
contradictions.counter: Rubric effectiveness is model- and benchmark-dependent, while ordinary panel means can remain brittle under correlated or biased contamination. deep-review needs human calibration; deep-ai-council needs diversity diagnostics, robust aggregation, and abstention.

contradictions.claim: runtime/convergence can treat persistently low marginal novelty as evidence that research should stop.
contradictions.counter: Low yield may only mean the current lane is exhausted; singleton-heavy topic or source-family counts can indicate substantial unseen mass and therefore require a pivot.

contradictions.claim: Maximizing semantic diversity necessarily broadens useful coverage.
contradictions.counter: DPP and disparity objectives can favor irrelevant outliers, while set-cover objectives can reward shallow label coverage; relevance, evidence quality and source-authority constraints must remain hard gates.

contradictions.claim: A formal alpha-nDCG score makes novelty objective.
contradictions.counter: Its result still depends on the aspect ontology and clustering version; splitting one aspect into several labels manufactures apparent novelty, so cluster lineage and sensitivity analysis are required.

contradictions.claim: Durable activity execution provides exactly-once LLM and tool effects.
contradictions.counter: Activities are generally at-least-once when execution finishes before its result is committed; runtime/locks-recovery needs an explicit indeterminate state, idempotency, and reconciliation.

contradictions.claim: Reissuing the same prompt during recovery is deterministic replay.
contradictions.counter: Replay returns the recorded assistant message; reissuing inference is a new stochastic attempt and must create a new branch or attempt identity in runtime/state-jsonl-checkpointing.

contradictions.claim: Temperature zero or a fixed seed makes an LLM response reproducible.
contradictions.counter: Greedy outputs can still diverge with GPU, batching, and reduction-order changes; runtime/gauges-observability should treat these settings as provenance, not a guarantee.

contradictions.claim: A renewable lease guarantees one active loop writer.
contradictions.counter: A paused former holder can resume after lease expiry; runtime/locks-recovery is safe only when canonical sinks reject stale fencing epochs.

contradictions.claim: Reflexion's 91% HumanEval result shows that verbal self-reflection reliably improves agents.
contradictions.counter: Reflection without generated tests scored 52% versus a 60% base; tests plus reflection reached 68%. External evidence quality is load-bearing for deep-improvement.

contradictions.claim: Self-Refine improves task performance by roughly 20 absolute points on average.
contradictions.counter: Math gains were nearly flat, and a resource-strong one-shot prompt scored 81.8 versus 75.1 after a seven-call refinement workflow; runtime/budget-cost needs matched-call baselines.

contradictions.claim: LLMs cannot intrinsically self-correct.
contradictions.counter: Neutral zero-temperature prompts show small, fragile gains, while trained policies such as SCoRe show substantial gains. Reflection policy and correction capability are distinct for deep-improvement.

contradictions.claim: Adding external feedback is sufficient to make iterative reflection safe.
contradictions.counter: Contextual drag persisted despite external feedback and successful verification; runtime/continuity-threading must also remove or quarantine the failed rationale.

contradictions.claim: Iterative debate itself improves collective correctness.
contradictions.counter: Across seven benchmarks, majority voting accounted for most observed gains, and unbiased debate produced no expected correctness improvement under the paper's belief-update model.

contradictions.claim: Heterogeneous model diversity is generally critical to debate gains.
contradictions.counter: ReConcile found diversity critical and reported large gains, whereas a newer matched-scaling study found little mathematical benefit from diversity; the effect appears task- and risk-dependent.

contradictions.claim: More rounds allow agents to progressively correct one another.
contradictions.counter: Longer discussions can drift, repeat low-quality feedback, and amplify majority pressure, suppressing correct minority revisions.

contradictions.claim: Broadcasting the full transcript gives every agent the information needed for better decisions.
contradictions.counter: Full broadcasts introduce redundant and noisy messages; early DAR results favor retaining a small set of authentic disagreements.

contradictions.claim: Temperature zero and a fixed seed make an LLM call deterministically replayable.
contradictions.counter: Batch composition, scheduling, hardware, kernels, and software versions can alter logits before sampling; online vLLM explicitly provides no reproducibility guarantee.

contradictions.claim: Recorded-effect replay proves that the current model or provider still behaves identically.
contradictions.counter: Cassette replay proves orchestration behavior against frozen responses; detecting provider drift requires live calls and therefore restores cost and nondeterminism.

contradictions.claim: Maximum determinism should be the default for every deep-research iteration.
contradictions.counter: Broadening benefits from stochastic exploration; the stronger design is exact trace capture during normal runs and deterministic replay only for diagnosis, audit, or controlled comparison.

contradictions.claim: The secretary problem's 1/e observation rule gives a generally optimal loop length.
contradictions.counter: Its guarantee depends on a known horizon, uniformly random arrival, no recall, and a single best choice; deep loops have recall, adaptive ordering, correlated gains, and multiple acceptable terminal artifacts.

contradictions.claim: Stopping when confidence or convergence becomes high is sufficient.
contradictions.counter: A success-only threshold sends unsolvable or stalled tasks to the maximum budget; a separately calibrated futility threshold is needed to stop trajectories whose progress cannot justify the remaining compute.

contradictions.claim: A stable raw majority is enough to stop council sampling.
contradictions.counter: Optimal stopping depends on posterior leader stability, priors, and sampling cost; shared-model correlation further makes raw vote counts overstate effective evidence.

contradictions.claim: High council agreement is evidence that the answer is safe or correct.
contradictions.counter: A coordinated or correlated attack can increase agreement; agreement must be calibrated against matched attack-mode runs and independent evidence verification.

contradictions.claim: LLM-42-style verified speculation makes complete agent traces exactly replayable.
contradictions.counter: It verifies token emission under a controlled inference schedule; provider responses, tool effects, clocks, process scheduling, and external state remain separate nondeterministic boundaries.

contradictions.claim: A hash-chained JSONL can contain sensitive payloads and still support selective physical erasure without weakening verification.
contradictions.counter: Deleting or rewriting authenticated entries breaks the history; sensitive payloads require an erasable side store while the immutable ledger retains only non-sensitive commitments and redaction evidence.

contradictions.claim: Anytime-valid confidence sequences remain valid under arbitrary adaptive routing and correlated council observations.
contradictions.counter: Optional-stopping validity depends on the specified filtration and test-martingale assumptions; shared-model correlation and post-selection can invalidate a naively applied sequence.

contradictions.claim: Doubly robust estimation can learn any improved stop policy from existing loop traces.
contradictions.counter: It still requires action support and either a defensible outcome model or logging-policy model; deterministic historical choices, missing propensities, censoring, and unobserved confounding can make policy value unidentified.

contradictions.claim: Async RL training is production-ready and safe for use
contradictions.counter: Async training may affect stability — use only when throughput is critical and convergence is validated
contradictions.evidence: verl ships fully_async_policy as production feature vs OpenRLHF README warning about async stability; resolution is bounded staleness (verl one_step_off_policy) vs unbounded queue (OpenRLHF async_queue_size default)

contradictions.claim: Zero external data is needed for self-play reasoning improvement
contradictions.counter: AZR still requires seed datasets (data/ directory with prompted seeds) and improvements are highly model-dependent (Llama3.1 +3.2 vs Qwen2.5-14B-Coder +13.2 total avg)
contradictions.evidence: LeapLabTHU/Absolute-Zero-Reasoner README seeding section + results table showing model-dependent gains

contradictions.claim: Maintaining high entropy prevents reasoning collapse
contradictions.counter: Template collapse occurs with high entropy — the model generates diverse-looking but input-agnostic reasoning. Entropy is necessary but insufficient; mutual information I(X;Z) is the missing axis.
contradictions.evidence: RAGEN-2 template collapse diagnostics showing high H(Z|X) + low I(X;Z) regime

contradictions.claim: Agentic iterative loops assume more compute/retries eventually improves the result (convergence-by-accumulation).
contradictions.counter: Workflow engines empirically cap retries (Airflow max_retries=3) and halt the DAG for intervention rather than blindly retrying, because blind retries amplify load without progress.
contradictions.evidence: apache/airflow max_retries default; dagster-io/dagster failure semantics vs. reflection-loop 'keep looping' optimism already catalogued

contradictions.claim: A fan-in/polling gate should just poll for completed sub-tasks.
contradictions.counter: A poll-hold gate exhausts the worker pool and deadlocks when there are more waiters than slots; the production-correct design reschedules (yields the slot) between pokes.
contradictions.evidence: apache/airflow sensor poke-vs-reschedule historical footgun and fix

contradictions.claim: Concurrency (multi-writer parallel fan-out) is needed for throughput, so deep-loop should adopt true concurrent writes to its state log
contradictions.counter: Streaming exactly-once (Flink barriers, Kafka transactions) proves concurrency coordination is only net-positive at thousands of partitions; at agent-loop scale (3-8 sub-agents) the barrier/transaction overhead exceeds the latency saved — keep single-writer sequential JSONL with commutative merges
contradictions.evidence: Flink checkpoint overhead scales with operator count; Kafka exactly-once transactions add latency proportional to participant count

contradictions.claim: Pure event sourcing (append-only, reconstruct by replay, never mutate) is the clean checkpointing model
contradictions.counter: runtime/dedup-novelty needs mutable union-set membership tests; pure replay is O(N) per resume to rebuild the seen-set, contradicting the O(1) novelty-check requirement — snapshots resolve it but reintroduce staleness
contradictions.evidence: Pekko Persistence journal-replay cost vs snapshot-staleness tradeoff

contradictions.claim: A well-tuned feedback control loop should converge to the setpoint — stability (keep iterating, tightening gain) is the objective.
contradictions.counter: Resilience4j/Hystrix production doctrine: when failure rate exceeds threshold, OPEN the circuit and STOP calling — the correct action under distress is to suspend the loop, not strengthen the feedback.
contradictions.evidence: resilience4j CircuitBreaker state machine vs classical PID stability literature

contradictions.claim: Iterative self-improvement loops converge monotonically toward better agents via reflection
contradictions.counter: SICA's authors report high variance and path-dependence: early edits bias later ones, so a single seed's trajectory can regress and gains are stochastic
contradictions.evidence: MaximeRobeyns/self_improving_coding_agent README 'Things to work on'; arXiv:2504.15228

contradictions.claim: Loop convergence can be measured on the same trajectory that generated the improvement
contradictions.counter: SkillOpt requires a held-out validation set precisely because in-trajectory scores overfit; accepting edits on in-loop scores causes false convergence

contradictions.claim: 'Incremental insert avoids duplicate computation' (nano-graphrag marketing)
contradictions.counter: nano-graphrag's own README caveat: 'each insert re-computes ALL communities and re-generates ALL community reports' — the cost-saving is contradicted by the actual re-clustering cost
contradictions.evidence: gusye1234/nano-graphrag readme incremental-insert section + Issues note

contradictions.claim: Schema-free GraphRAG accumulation improves answer quality over iterations (LightRAG positioning)
contradictions.counter: Schema-free OpenIE noise degrades quality unless fixed by schema-constrained + semantic-alignment construction
contradictions.evidence: OpenSPG/KAG paper arXiv:2409.13731 'alleviate the noise problem caused by OpenIE' vs HKUDS/LightRAG set-merge gains

contradictions.claim: More/heavier offline indexing yields better accumulated memory
contradictions.counter: HippoRAG 2 uses 'significantly fewer offline indexing resources than GraphRAG/RAPTOR/LightRAG' yet wins continual-learning benchmarks

contradictions.claim: EDD guidance (Anthropic-derived, echoed by Red Hat/Lushbinary/Medium) says evaluation is THE outer loop driving every choice — always-on, measurement-gated, eval-first.
contradictions.counter: Eureka achieves human-level reward design with eval as a fitness signal inside evolutionary search, NOT as a held-out gate — and its eval is environment execution, not a test suite.

contradictions.claim: ChatDev Puppeteer: a learnable RL-optimized central orchestrator improves multi-agent collaboration AND reduces computational cost.
contradictions.counter: An RL-learned orchestrator is itself a loop that must converge; it inherits the SICA path-dependence / multi-seed variance problem (open thread), so 'reduces cost' is conditional on the meta-loop having converged — exactly the unsolved problem.
contradictions.evidence: arXiv 2505.19591 vs prior debate-hurts / path-dependence findings

contradictions.claim: Adding a more capable model/participant to a critique loop monotonically improves output quality (assumption behind deep-improvement & deep-ai-council).
contradictions.counter: Weak-to-strong: a stronger model naively trained on weaker-model labels can UNDERPERFORM the weak supervisor (negative transfer / sycophancy) unless an anti-overconfidence auxiliary loss is added.

contradictions.claim: To improve loop results, increase iteration depth (refine the same candidate further).
contradictions.counter: Red-team iterate loops (PAIR/TAP) show success saturates within ~20 queries and that aggressive fan-out-with-pruning at shallow depth beats deeper refinement.
contradictions.evidence: PAIR arXiv:2310.08419; TAP arXiv:2312.02119 depth/width ablations

contradictions.claim: A single LLM-as-judge is a sufficient evaluator/critic inside a loop.
contradictions.counter: HarmBench & RewardBench both ship/require a SECOND held-out judge (validation classifier / ensemble) because the primary judge is itself attackable and reward models over-optimizable; single-judge loops Goodhart their own metric.
contradictions.evidence: HarmBench validation classifier; RewardBench RM ensembling + over-optimization literature (Gao 2022)

contradictions.claim: Multi-agent debate/council monotonically improves truth-tracking.
contradictions.counter: Gains vanish for homogeneous same-family ensembles; a single strong model often beats many correlated weak ones.
contradictions.evidence: catalogue open thread + selfcheckgpt correlated-blind-spot rationale

contradictions.claim: Self-reflection/self-refine loops reliably self-correct.
contradictions.counter: A model cannot reliably catch errors it produced without an external/cross-sample signal; gains often come from extra compute/rewinds.

contradictions.claim: Checkpoint/resume = deterministic durable replay.
contradictions.counter: Under concurrent fan-out, single-writer replay breaks; you need CRDT-style conflict-free merge.
contradictions.evidence: automerge/automerge CRDT vs single-writer replay assumption

contradictions.claim: Borrow a mature external loop engine (e.g. onefuzz) as the runtime substrate.
contradictions.counter: onefuzz is archived (Nov 2023); architectural lessons survive but a hard runtime coupling is a liability.

contradictions.claim: In-place continuous knowledge editing with normalization (UltraEdit/WISE) is the efficient way to accumulate cross-iteration knowledge.
contradictions.counter: EasyEdit's own locality metrics show catastrophic forgetting / edit-interference across thousands of edits, so append-only is safer for durability.
contradictions.evidence: zjunlp/EasyEdit lifelong-editing locality degradation + GRACE rollback tradeoffs

contradictions.claim: Delay-based concurrency backoff (Vegas) optimally protects fan-out from overload.
contradictions.counter: LLM latency is dominated by output-token length and provider routing, not real queuing, so delay-based backoff over-reacts to long reasoning traces; loss-based (AIMD, trigger on reject/timeout) is more robust.
contradictions.evidence: concurrency-limits Gradient2 design rationale (fixes Vegas min-RTT drift)
